#### <span id="page-46-0"></span>**Requirement 4: Encrypt transmission of cardholder data across open, public networks**

Sensitive information must be encrypted during transmission over networks that are easily accessed by malicious individuals. Misconfigured wireless networks and vulnerabilities in legacy encryption and authentication protocols continue to be targets of malicious individuals who exploit these vulnerabilities to gain privileged access to cardholder data environments.

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 4.1 Use strong cryptography and security<br>protocols to safeguard sensitive cardholder<br>data<br>during transmission over open, public<br>networks, including the following:<br>•<br>Only trusted keys and certificates are | Identify all locations where cardholder data is<br>4.1.a<br>transmitted or received over open, public networks.<br>Examine<br>documented standards and compare to system<br>configurations to verify the use of security protocols and<br>strong cryptography for all locations.                                                 | Sensitive information must be encrypted during<br>transmission over public networks, because it is<br>easy and common for a malicious individual to<br>intercept and/or divert data while in transit.<br>Secure transmission of cardholder data requires                                                       |
| accepted.<br>•<br>The protocol in use only supports<br>secure versions or configurations.<br>•<br>The encryption strength is appropriate<br>for the encryption methodology in use.                                            | Review documented policies and procedures to verify<br>4.1.b<br>processes are specified for the following:<br>•<br>For acceptance of only trusted keys and/or certificates<br>•<br>For the protocol in use to only support secure versions<br>and configurations (that insecure versions or<br>configurations are not supported) | using trusted keys/certificates, a secure protocol<br>for transport, and proper encryption strength to<br>encrypt cardholder data. Connection requests<br>from systems that do not support the required<br>encryption strength, and that would result in an<br>insecure connection,<br>should not be accepted. |
| Examples of open, public networks include<br>but are not limited to:                                                                                                                                                          | •<br>For implementation of proper encryption strength per<br>the encryption methodology in use                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Note that some protocol implementations (such as<br>SSL, SSH v1.0,<br>and early TLS) have known<br>vulnerabilities that an attacker can use to gain                                                                                                                                                            |
| •<br>The Internet<br>•<br>Wireless technologies, including 802.11<br>and Bluetooth<br>•<br>Cellular technologies, for example,<br>Global System for Mobile                                                                    | Select and observe a sample of inbound and outbound<br>4.1.c<br>transmissions as they occur (for example, by observing<br>system processes or network traffic) to verify that all<br>cardholder data is encrypted with strong cryptography<br>during transit.                                                                    | control of the affected system. Whichever security<br>protocol is used, ensure it is configured<br>to use<br>only secure versions and configurations to prevent<br>use of an insecure connection—for example, by<br>using only trusted certificates and supporting only                                        |
| communications (GSM), Code division<br>multiple access (CDMA)<br>•<br>General Packet Radio Service (GPRS)<br>•<br>Satellite communications                                                                                    | Examine keys and certificates to verify that only<br>4.1.d<br>trusted keys and/or certificates are accepted.                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | strong encryption (not supporting weaker,<br>insecure protocols or methods).<br>Verifying that certificates are trusted (for example,                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Examine system configurations to verify that the<br>4.1.e<br>protocol is implemented to use only secure configurations<br>and does not support insecure versions or configurations.                                                                                                                                              | have not expired and are issued from a trusted<br>source)<br>helps ensure the integrity of the secure<br>connection.                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Examine system configurations to verify that the proper<br>4.1.f<br>encryption strength is implemented for the encryption<br>methodology in use. (Check vendor recommendations/best<br>practices.)                                                                                                                               | (Continued on next page)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                       | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|                                                                                                                                                                                                            | For TLS implementations,<br>examine system<br>4.1.g<br>configurations to verify that TLS is enabled whenever<br>cardholder data is transmitted or received.<br>For example, for browser-based implementations:<br>•<br>"HTTPS"<br>appears as the browser Universal Record<br>Locator (URL) protocol, and<br>•<br>Cardholder data is only requested if "HTTPS"<br>appears<br>as part of the URL. | Generally, the web page URL should begin with<br>"HTTPS" and/or the web browser display a<br>padlock icon somewhere in the window of the<br>browser. Many TLS certificate vendors also<br>provide a highly visible verification seal—<br>sometimes referred to as a "security seal,"<br>"secure site seal," or "secure trust seal")—which<br>may provide the ability to click on the seal to<br>reveal information about the website.<br>Refer to industry standards and best practices for<br>information on strong cryptography and secure<br>protocols (e.g.,<br>NIST SP 800-52 and SP 800-57,<br>OWASP, etc.)<br>Note:<br>SSL/early TLS is not considered strong<br>cryptography and may not be used as a security<br>control, except by POS POI terminals that are<br>verified as not being susceptible to known exploits<br>and the termination points to which they connect<br>as defined in Appendix<br>A2. |
| 4.1.1 Ensure wireless networks transmitting<br>cardholder data or connected to the<br>cardholder data environment, use industry<br>best practices to implement strong<br>encryption for authentication and | Identify all wireless networks transmitting cardholder<br>4.1.1<br>data or connected to the cardholder data environment.<br>Examine documented standards and compare to system<br>configuration settings to verify the following for all wireless<br>networks identified:                                                                                                                       | Malicious users use free and widely available<br>tools to eavesdrop on wireless communications.<br>Use of strong cryptography can help limit<br>disclosure of sensitive information across wireless<br>networks.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| transmission.                                                                                                                                                                                              | •<br>Industry best practices are used to implement strong<br>encryption for authentication and transmission.<br>•<br>Weak encryption (for example, WEP, SSL) is not<br>used as a security control for authentication or<br>transmission.                                                                                                                                                        | Strong cryptography for authentication and<br>transmission of cardholder data is required to<br>prevent malicious users from gaining access to<br>the wireless network or utilizing wireless networks<br>to access other internal networks or data.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                   | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Never send unprotected PANs by end<br>4.2<br>user messaging technologies (for example, e<br>mail, instant messaging, SMS, chat, etc.).                                                 | If end-user messaging technologies are used to send<br>4.2.a<br>cardholder data, observe processes for sending PAN and<br>examine<br>a sample of outbound transmissions as they occur<br>to verify that PAN is rendered unreadable or secured with<br>strong cryptography whenever it is sent via end-user<br>messaging technologies. | E-mail, instant messaging, SMS, and chat can be<br>easily intercepted by packet-sniffing during<br>delivery across internal and public networks. |                                                                                                                                                                                        | Review written policies to verify the existence of a<br>4.2.b<br>policy stating that unprotected PANs are not to be sent via<br>end-user messaging technologies.                                                                                                                                                                      | Additionally, if an entity requests PAN via end<br>user messaging technologies, the entity should<br>provide a tool or method to protect these PANs<br>using strong cryptography or render PANs<br>unreadable before transmission.                                  |

{49}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_49_Picture_0.jpeg)

### <span id="page-49-0"></span>**Maintain a Vulnerability Management Program**

#### <span id="page-49-1"></span>*Requirement 5: Protect all systems against malware and regularly update anti-virus software or programs*

Malicious software, commonly referred to as "malware"—including viruses, worms, and Trojans—enters the network during many businessapproved activities including employee e-mail and use of the Internet, mobile computers, and storage devices, resulting in the exploitation of system vulnerabilities. Anti-virus software must be used on all systems commonly affected by malware to protect systems from current and evolving malicious software threats. Additional anti-malware solutions may be considered as a supplement to the anti-virus software; however, such additional solutions do not replace the need for anti-virus software to be in place.

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                             | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 5.1<br>Deploy anti-virus software on all<br>systems commonly affected by malicious<br>software (particularly personal computers<br>and servers). | 5.1<br>For<br>a sample of system components including all operating<br>system types commonly affected by malicious software, verify<br>that anti-virus software is deployed if applicable anti-virus<br>technology exists.                                                                                                                                                                                                 | There is a constant stream of attacks using widely<br>published exploits, often called "zero day" (an<br>attack that exploits a previously unknown<br>vulnerability), against otherwise secured systems.<br>Without an anti-virus solution that is updated<br>regularly, these new forms of malicious software<br>can attack systems, disable a network, or lead to<br>compromise of data. |
| 5.1.1 Ensure that anti-virus programs<br>are capable of detecting, removing,<br>and protecting against all known types<br>of malicious software. | Review<br>vendor documentation and examine anti-virus<br>5.1.1<br>configurations to verify that anti-virus programs;<br>•<br>Detect<br>all known types of malicious software,<br>•<br>Remove<br>all known types of malicious software, and<br>•<br>Protect<br>against all known types of malicious software.<br>Examples of types of malicious software include viruses,<br>Trojans, worms, spyware, adware, and rootkits. | It is important to protect against ALL<br>types and<br>forms of malicious software.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

{50}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_50_Picture_0.jpeg)

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| For systems considered to be not<br>5.1.2<br>commonly affected by malicious<br>software,<br>perform periodic evaluations<br>to identify and evaluate evolving<br>malware threats in order to confirm<br>whether such systems continue to not<br>require anti-virus software. | 5.1.2 Interview personnel to verify that evolving malware<br>threats are monitored and evaluated for systems not currently<br>considered to be commonly affected by malicious software, in<br>order to confirm whether such systems<br>continue to not require<br>anti-virus software. | Typically, mainframes, mid-range computers<br>(such as AS/400) and similar systems may not<br>currently be commonly targeted or affected by<br>malware. However, industry trends for malicious<br>software can change quickly, so it is<br>important for<br>organizations to be aware of new malware that<br>might affect their systems—for example, by<br>monitoring vendor security notices and anti-virus<br>news groups to determine whether<br>their systems<br>might be coming under threat from new and<br>evolving malware.<br>Trends in malicious software should be included<br>in the identification of new security vulnerabilities,<br>and methods to address new trends should be<br>incorporated into the company's configuration<br>standards and protection mechanisms as needed |
| 5.2 Ensure that all anti-virus mechanisms<br>are maintained as follows:                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Examine policies and procedures to verify that anti-virus<br>5.2.a<br>software and definitions<br>are required to be kept up to date.                                                                                                                                                  | Even the best anti-virus solutions are limited in<br>effectiveness if they are not maintained and kept                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| •<br>Are kept current,<br>•<br>Perform periodic scans                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Examine anti-virus configurations, including the master<br>5.2.b<br>installation of the software to verify anti-virus mechanisms are:                                                                                                                                                  | current with the latest security updates, signature<br>files,<br>or malware protections.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| •<br>Generate audit logs which are<br>retained per PCI DSS Requirement<br>10.7.                                                                                                                                                                                              | •<br>Configured<br>to perform automatic updates, and<br>•<br>Configured to<br>perform periodic scans.                                                                                                                                                                                  | Audit logs provide the ability to monitor virus and<br>malware activity and anti-malware reactions.<br>Thus, it is imperative that anti-malware solutions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Examine a sample of system components, including all<br>5.2.c<br>operating system types commonly affected by malicious<br>software, to verify that:                                                                                                                                    | be configured to generate audit logs and that<br>these logs be managed in accordance with<br>Requirement 10.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | •<br>The anti-virus software and definitions are current.<br>•<br>Periodic<br>scans<br>are performed.                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |

|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | •<br>Anti-virus software log generation is enabled, and                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | •<br>Logs are retained<br>in accordance with PCI DSS<br>Requirement<br>10.7.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |

|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Ensure that anti-virus mechanisms<br>5.3<br>are actively running and cannot be<br>disabled or altered by users, unless                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Examine anti-virus configurations, including the master<br>5.3.a<br>installation of the software and a sample of system<br>components,<br>to verify the anti-virus software is<br>actively running.                                                                                 | Anti-virus that continually runs and is unable to be<br>altered will provide persistent security against<br>malware.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |

| Anti-virus solutions may be<br>Note:<br>temporarily disabled only if there is<br>legitimate technical need, as authorized<br>by management on a case-by-case<br>basis. If anti-virus protection needs to be<br>disabled for a specific purpose, it must<br>be formally authorized. Additional<br>security measures may also need to be<br>implemented for the period of time during<br>which anti-virus protection is not active. | disabled or altered by users.<br>Interview responsible personnel and observe processes to<br>5.3.c<br>verify that anti-virus software cannot be disabled or altered by<br>users, unless specifically authorized by management on a<br>case-by-case basis for a limited time period. | from being exploited by malicious software.<br>Additional security measures may also need to be<br>implemented for the period of time during which<br>anti-virus protection is not active—for example,<br>disconnecting<br>the unprotected system from the<br>Internet while<br>the<br>anti-virus protection<br>is disabled,<br>and running a full scan after it is re-enabled. |

{52}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_52_Picture_0.jpeg)

#### <span id="page-52-0"></span>*Requirement 6: Develop and maintain secure systems and applications*

Unscrupulous individuals use security vulnerabilities to gain privileged access to systems. Many of these vulnerabilities are fixed by vendorprovided security patches, which must be installed by the entities that manage the systems. All systems must have all appropriate software patches to protect against the exploitation and compromise of cardholder data by malicious individuals and malicious software.

*Note: Appropriate software patches are those patches that have been evaluated and tested sufficiently to determine that the patches do not conflict with existing security configurations. For in-house developed applications, numerous vulnerabilities can be avoided by using standard system development processes and secure coding techniques.*

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 6.1 Establish a process to identify security<br>vulnerabilities, using reputable outside<br>sources for security vulnerability information,<br>and assign a risk ranking (for example, as<br>"high,"<br>"medium,"<br>or "low") to newly<br>discovered security vulnerabilities.<br>Note: Risk rankings should be based on                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | 6.1.a Examine policies and procedures to verify that<br>processes are defined for the following:<br>•<br>To identify new security vulnerabilities<br>•<br>To assign a risk ranking to vulnerabilities that includes<br>identification of all "high risk"<br>and "critical"<br>vulnerabilities.<br>•<br>To use<br>reputable outside sources for security                                                                                                                 | The intent of this requirement is that organizations<br>keep up<br>to<br>date with new vulnerabilities that may<br>impact their environment.<br>Sources for vulnerability information should be<br>trustworthy and often include vendor websites,<br>industry news groups, mailing list,<br>or RSS feeds.<br>Once an organization identifies a vulnerability that                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| industry best practices as well as<br>consideration of potential impact. For<br>example, criteria for ranking vulnerabilities<br>may include consideration of the CVSS base<br>score, and/or the classification by the vendor,<br>and/or type of systems affected.<br>Methods for evaluating vulnerabilities and<br>assigning risk ratings will vary based on an<br>organization's environment and risk<br>assessment strategy. Risk rankings should,<br>at a<br>minimum, identify all vulnerabilities<br>considered to be a "high risk" to the<br>environment. In addition to the risk ranking,<br>vulnerabilities may be considered "critical" if<br>they pose an imminent threat to the<br>environment, impact critical systems, and/or<br>would result in a potential compromise if not<br>addressed. Examples of critical systems may<br>include security systems, public-facing<br>devices and systems, databases, and other<br>systems that store, process,<br>or transmit<br>cardholder data. | vulnerability information.<br>6.1.b<br>Interview responsible personnel and observe<br>processes to verify that:<br>•<br>New security vulnerabilities are identified.<br>•<br>A risk ranking is assigned to vulnerabilities that includes<br>identification of all "high<br>risk"<br>and "critical"<br>vulnerabilities.<br>•<br>Processes to identify new security vulnerabilities include<br>using reputable outside sources for security vulnerability<br>information. | could affect their environment, the risk that the<br>vulnerability poses must be evaluated and ranked.<br>The organization must therefore have a method in<br>place to evaluate vulnerabilities on an ongoing basis<br>and assign risk rankings to those vulnerabilities.<br>This is not achieved by an ASV scan or internal<br>vulnerability scan, rather this requires a process to<br>actively monitor industry sources for vulnerability<br>information.<br>Classifying the risks (for example, as "high,"<br>"medium,"<br>or "low") allows organizations to identify,<br>prioritize,<br>and address the highest risk items more<br>quickly and reduce the likelihood that vulnerabilities<br>posing the greatest risk will be exploited. |

| 6.2 Ensure that all system components and<br>software<br>are protected from known<br>vulnerabilities by installing applicable vendor<br>supplied security patches. Install critical<br>security patches within one month of release.<br>Critical security patches should be<br>Note:<br>identified according to the risk ranking | 6.2.a<br>Examine policies and procedures related to security<br>patch installation to verify processes are defined for:<br>•<br>Installation of applicable critical vendor-supplied<br>security patches within one month of release.<br>•<br>Installation of all applicable vendor-supplied security<br>patches within an appropriate time frame (for example,<br>within three months).                                                                                          | There is a constant stream of attacks using widely<br>published exploits, often called "zero day" (an attack<br>that exploits a previously unknown vulnerability),<br>against otherwise secured systems. If the most<br>recent patches are not implemented on critical<br>systems as soon as possible, a malicious individual<br>can use these exploits to attack or disable a system,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| process defined in Requirement 6.1.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | For a sample of system components and related<br>6.2.b<br>software, compare the list of security patches installed on<br>each system to the most recent vendor security-patch list, to<br>verify the following:<br>•<br>That applicable critical vendor-supplied security patches<br>are installed within one month of release.<br>•<br>All applicable vendor-supplied security patches are<br>installed within an appropriate time frame (for example,<br>within three months). | or gain access to sensitive data.<br>Prioritizing patches for critical infrastructure ensures<br>that high-priority systems and devices are protected<br>from vulnerabilities as soon as possible after a patch<br>is released. Consider prioritizing patch installations<br>such that security patches for critical or at-risk<br>systems are installed within 30 days, and other<br>lower-risk patches are installed within 2-3 months.<br>This requirement applies to applicable patches for<br>all installed software, including payment applications<br>(both those that are PA-DSS validated<br>and those<br>that are not). |
| 6.3<br>Develop internal and external software<br>applications (including web-based<br>administrative access to applications)                                                                                                                                                                                                     | 6.3.a Examine written software-development processes to<br>verify that the processes are based on industry standards<br>and/or best practices.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Without the inclusion of security during the<br>requirements definition, design, analysis, and testing<br>phases of software development, security                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| securely, as follows:<br>•<br>In accordance with PCI DSS (for<br>example, secure authentication and<br>logging)                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 6.3.b Examine written software-development processes to<br>verify that information security is included throughout the life<br>cycle.<br>Examine written software-development processes to<br>6.3.c<br>verify that software applications are developed in<br>accordance with PCI DSS.<br>Interview software developers to verify that written<br>6.3.d<br>software-development processes are implemented.                                                                        | vulnerabilities can be inadvertently or maliciously<br>introduced into the production environment.<br>Understanding how sensitive data is handled by the<br>application—including when stored, transmitted, and                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| •<br>Based on industry standards and/or best<br>practices.<br>•<br>Incorporating<br>information security                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | when in memory—can help identify where data<br>needs to be protected.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| throughout the software-development life<br>cycle                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Note: this applies to all software developed<br>internally as well as bespoke or custom<br>software developed by a third party.                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 6.3.1 Remove development, test and/or<br>custom application accounts, user IDs, and<br>passwords before applications become<br>active or are released to<br>customers.                                                                                                                                                                                                         | 6.3.1 Examine written software-development procedures<br>and interview responsible personnel to verify that pre<br>production and/or custom application accounts, user IDs<br>and/or passwords are removed before an application goes<br>into production or is released to customers.                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Development, test and/or custom application<br>accounts, user IDs, and passwords should be<br>removed from production code before the<br>application becomes active or is released to<br>customers, since these items may give away<br>information about the functioning of the application.<br>Possession of such information could facilitate<br>compromise of the application and related<br>cardholder data.                                                                                                                                                            |
| 6.3.2 Review custom code prior to release<br>to production or customers in order to<br>identify any potential coding vulnerability<br>(using either manual or automated                                                                                                                                                                                                        | 6.3.2.a Examine written software-development procedures<br>and interview responsible personnel to verify that all<br>custom application code changes must be reviewed (using<br>either manual or automated processes) as follows:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Security vulnerabilities in custom code are<br>commonly exploited by malicious individuals to gain<br>access to a network and compromise cardholder<br>data.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| processes) to include at least the following:<br>•<br>Code changes are reviewed by<br>individuals other than the originating<br>code author, and by individuals<br>knowledgeable about code-review<br>techniques and secure coding<br>practices.<br>•<br>Code reviews ensure code is<br>developed according to secure coding<br>guidelines<br>•<br>Appropriate corrections are | •<br>Code changes are reviewed by individuals other than<br>the originating code author, and by individuals who<br>are knowledgeable in code-review techniques and<br>secure coding practices.<br>•<br>Code reviews ensure code is developed according to<br>secure coding guidelines (see PCI DSS Requirement<br>6.5).<br>•<br>Appropriate<br>corrections are implemented prior to<br>release.<br>•<br>Code-review results are reviewed and approved by<br>management prior to release. | An individual knowledgeable and experienced in<br>code-review techniques should be involved in the<br>review process. Code reviews should be performed<br>by someone other than the developer of the code to<br>allow for an independent, objective review.<br>Automated tools or processes may also be used in<br>lieu of manual reviews, but keep in mind that it may<br>be difficult or even impossible for an automated tool<br>to identify some coding issues.<br>Correcting coding errors before the code is deployed<br>into a production environment or released to |
| implemented prior to release.<br>•<br>Code-review results are reviewed and<br>approved by management prior to<br>release.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | customers prevents the code exposing the<br>environments to potential exploit. Faulty code is also<br>far more difficult and expensive to address after it<br>has been deployed or released into production<br>environments.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| (Continued on next page)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Including a formal review and signoff by<br>management prior to release helps to ensure that<br>code is approved and has been developed in<br>accordance with policies and procedures.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Note:<br>This requirement for code reviews<br>applies to all custom code (both internal and<br>public-facing), as part of the system<br>development life cycle.<br>Code reviews can be conducted by<br>knowledgeable internal personnel or third<br>parties. Public-facing web<br>applications are<br>also subject to additional controls, to address<br>ongoing threats and vulnerabilities after<br>implementation, as defined at PCI DSS<br>Requirement 6.6. | 6.3.2.b Select a sample of recent custom application<br>changes and verify that custom application code is<br>reviewed according to 6.3.2.a, above.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Follow change control processes and<br>6.4<br>procedures for all changes to system<br>components. The processes must include the<br>following:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 6.4 Examine policies and procedures to verify the following<br>are defined:<br>•<br>Development/test environments are separate from<br>production environments with access control in place to<br>enforce separation.<br>•<br>A separation of duties between personnel assigned to<br>the development/test environments and those assigned<br>to the production environment.<br>•<br>Production data (live PANs) are not used for testing or<br>development.<br>•<br>Test data and accounts are removed before a<br>production system becomes active.<br>•<br>Change<br>control procedures related to implementing<br>security patches and software modifications are<br>documented. | Without properly documented and implemented<br>change controls, security features could be<br>inadvertently or deliberately omitted or rendered<br>inoperable, processing irregularities could occur, or<br>malicious code could be introduced.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 6.4.1 Separate development/test<br>environments from production<br>environments, and enforce the separation<br>with access controls.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | 6.4.1.a Examine network documentation and network<br>device configurations to verify that the development/test<br>environments are separate from the production<br>environment(s).<br>6.4.1.b Examine access controls settings to verify that<br>access controls are in place to enforce separation<br>between the development/test environments and the<br>production environment(s).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Due to the constantly changing state of development<br>and test environments, they tend to be less secure<br>than the production environment. Without adequate<br>separation between environments, it may be<br>possible for the production environment, and<br>cardholder data, to be compromised due to less<br>stringent security configurations and possible<br>vulnerabilities in a test or development environment. |

|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 6.4.2 Separation of duties between<br>development/test and production<br>environments                                                  | 6.4.2 Observe processes and interview personnel<br>assigned to development/test environments and personnel<br>assigned to production environments to verify that<br>separation of duties is in place between development/test<br>environments and the production environment. | Reducing the number of personnel with access to<br>the production environment and cardholder data<br>minimizes risk and helps ensure that access is<br>limited to those individuals with a business need to<br>know.                                                                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | The intent of this requirement is to separate<br>development and test functions from production<br>functions. Use of production<br>data provides malicious individuals with the<br>opportunity to gain unauthorized access to                                                                                                                               |

| 6.4.4 Removal of test data and accounts<br>from system<br>components before the system<br>becomes<br>active<br>/ goes into production. | 6.4.4.a Observe testing processes and interview<br>personnel<br>to verify test data and accounts are removed<br>before a production system becomes active.                                                                                                                    | Test data and accounts should be removed before<br>the system component becomes active<br>(in<br>production), since these items may give away                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                                                                                                                                        | 6.4.4.b Examine a sample of data and accounts from<br>production systems recently installed or updated to verify<br>test data and accounts are removed before the system<br>becomes active.                                                                                   | information about the functioning of the application<br>or system.
