| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| If a session has been idle for<br>8.1.8<br>more than 15 minutes, require the user<br>to re-authenticate to re-activate the<br>terminal or session.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | For a sample of system components, inspect system<br>8.1.8<br>configuration settings to verify that system/session idle time out<br>features have been set to 15 minutes or less.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | When users walk away from an open machine with<br>access to critical system components or cardholder<br>data, that machine may be used by others in the<br>user's absence, resulting in unauthorized account<br>access and/or misuse.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | The re-authentication can be applied either at the<br>system level to protect all sessions running on that<br>machine, or at the application level.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| 8.2<br>In addition to assigning a unique ID,<br>ensure proper user-authentication<br>management for non-consumer users<br>and administrators on all system<br>components by employing at least one of<br>the following methods to authenticate all<br>users:<br>•<br>Something you know, such as a<br>password or passphrase<br>•<br>Something you have, such as a<br>token device or smart card<br>•<br>Something you are, such as a<br>biometric. | 8.2<br>To verify that users are authenticated using unique ID and<br>additional authentication (for example, a password/phrase) for<br>access to the cardholder data environment, perform the<br>following:<br>•<br>Examine documentation describing the authentication<br>method(s) used.<br>•<br>For each type of authentication method used and for each<br>type of system component, observe an authentication to<br>verify authentication is functioning consistent with<br>documented authentication method(s). | These authentication methods, when used in<br>addition to unique IDs, help protect users' IDs from<br>being compromised, since the one attempting the<br>compromise needs to know both the unique ID and<br>the password (or other authentication used). Note<br>that a digital certificate is a valid option for<br>"something you have" as long as it is unique for a<br>particular user.<br>Since one of the first steps a malicious individual will<br>take to compromise a system is to exploit weak or<br>nonexistent passwords, it is important to implement<br>good processes for authentication management. |

| Using strong cryptography,<br>8.2.1<br>render all authentication credentials<br>(such as passwords/phrases)<br>unreadable during transmission and<br>storage on all system components. | 8.2.1.a Examine vendor documentation and system<br>configuration settings to verify that passwords are protected<br>with strong cryptography during transmission and storage.                                                                                                                                                                           | Many network devices and applications transmit<br>unencrypted, readable passwords across the<br>network and/or store passwords without encryption.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                                                                        | For a sample of system components, examine<br>8.2.1.b<br>password files to verify that passwords are unreadable during<br>storage.                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | A malicious individual can easily intercept<br>unencrypted passwords during transmission using a<br>"sniffer," or directly access unencrypted passwords<br>in files where they are stored, and use this data to                                                                                                                                                                    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                        | For a sample of system components, examine data<br>8.2.1.c                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | gain unauthorized access.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                                                                        | transmissions to<br>verify that passwords are unreadable during<br>transmission.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Note: Testing Procedures 8.2.1.d and 8.2.1.e are<br>additional procedures<br>that only apply if the entity                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                                                                        | 8.2.1.d<br>Additional testing procedure for service provider<br>Observe<br>password files to verify that non<br>assessments only:<br>consumer customer passwords are unreadable during storage.                                                                                                                                                         | being assessed is a service provider.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                                                                                                                                                                                        | 8.2.1.e<br>Additional testing procedure for service provider<br>Observe<br>data transmissions to verify that<br>assessments only:<br>non-consumer customer passwords are unreadable during<br>transmission.                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Verify user identity before<br>8.2.2<br>modifying any authentication<br>credential—for example, performing<br>password resets, provisioning new<br>tokens, or generating new keys.     | Examine authentication procedures for modifying<br>8.2.2<br>authentication credentials and observe security personnel to<br>verify that, if a user requests a reset of an authentication<br>credential by phone, e-mail, web, or other non-face-to-face<br>method, the user's identity is verified before the authentication<br>credential is modified. | Many malicious individuals use "social<br>engineering"—for example, calling a help desk and<br>acting as a legitimate user—to have a password<br>changed so they can utilize a user ID. Consider use<br>of a "secret question" that only the proper user can<br>answer to help administrators identify the<br>user prior<br>to re-setting or modifying authentication credentials. |

| Passwords/passphrases must<br>8.2.3<br>meet the following:<br>•<br>Require a minimum length of at<br>least seven characters.<br>•<br>Contain both numeric and<br>alphabetic characters.<br>Alternatively, the passwords/<br>passphrases<br>must have complexity<br>and strength at least equivalent to the<br>parameters specified above. | For a sample of system components, inspect system<br>8.2.3a<br>configuration settings to verify that user password/passphrase<br>parameters are set to require at least the following<br>strength/complexity:<br>•<br>Require a minimum length of at least seven characters.<br>•<br>Contain both numeric and alphabetic characters.<br>8.2.3.b<br>Additional testing procedure for service provider<br>assessments only:<br>Review<br>internal processes and<br>customer/user documentation to verify that non-consumer<br>customer passwords/passphrases<br>are required to meet at least<br>the following strength/complexity:<br>•<br>Require a<br>minimum length of at least seven characters.<br>•<br>Contain<br>both numeric and alphabetic characters. | Strong passwords/passphrases are the first line of<br>defense into a network since a malicious individual<br>will often first try to find accounts with weak or non<br>existent passwords. If passwords are short or simple<br>to guess, it<br>is relatively easy for a malicious<br>individual to find<br>these weak accounts and<br>compromise a network under the guise of a valid<br>user ID.<br>This requirement specifies that a minimum of seven<br>characters and both numeric and alphabetic<br>characters should be used for passwords/<br>passphrases. system<br>8.2.4.a<br>configuration settings to verify that user password/passphrase<br>parameters are set to require users to change passwords at<br>least once every 90 days. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 8.2.5.b<br>Additional testing procedure for service provider<br>Review internal processes and<br>assessments only:                                                                                                                                                            | that have been guessed or brute-forced will be used<br>in the future.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | customer/user documentation to verify that new non-consumer<br>customer<br>user passwords/passphrase<br>cannot be the same as<br>the previous four passwords.                                                                                                                 | Testing Procedure 8.2.5.b is an additional<br>Note:<br>procedure that only applies if the entity being<br>assessed is a service provider.                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| 8.2.6 Set passwords/passphrases for<br>first-time use and upon reset to a<br>unique value for each user, and<br>change immediately after the first use.                                                                                          | 8.2.6 Examine password procedures and observe security<br>personnel to verify that first-time passwords/passphrases<br>for<br>new users, and reset<br>passwords/passphrases<br>for existing<br>users, are set to a unique value for each user and changed<br>after first use. | If the same password is used for every new user, an<br>internal user, former employee, or malicious<br>individual may know or easily discover this<br>password, and use it to gain access to accounts.                                                                                                                                                   |
| 8.3 Secure all individual non-console<br>administrative<br>access and all remote<br>access to the CDE using multi-factor<br>authentication.                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Multi-factor authentication requires an individual to<br>present a minimum of two separate forms of<br>authentication<br>(as described in Requirement 8.2),<br>before access is granted.                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Multi-factor authentication requires<br>Note:<br>that<br>a minimum of<br>two of the three<br>authentication methods (see<br>Requirement 8.2 for descriptions of<br>authentication methods) be used for<br>authentication. Using one factor twice |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Multi-factor authentication provides additional<br>assurance that the individual attempting to gain<br>access is who they claim to be.<br>With<br>multi-factor<br>authentication, an attacker would need to<br>compromise at least two different authentication<br>mechanisms, increasing<br>the difficulty of compromise<br>and thus reducing the risk. |
| (for example, using two separate<br>passwords) is not considered multi-factor<br>authentication.                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Multi-factor authentication is not required at both the<br>system-level<br>and application-level for a particular<br>system component. Multi-factor authentication<br>can<br>be performed either upon authentication to the<br>particular network or to the system component.                                                                            |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Examples of multi-factor technologies include but<br>are not limited to remote authentication and dial-in<br>service (RADIUS) with tokens; terminal access<br>controller access control system (TACACS) with<br>tokens; and other technologies that facilitate multi<br>factor authentication.                                                           |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 8.3.1 Incorporate multi-factor<br>authentication for<br>all<br>non-console<br>access into the CDE for<br>personnel<br>with                                                                                                                                   | 8.3.1.a Examine network and/or system configurations, as<br>applicable, to verify multi-factor<br>authentication is required for all<br>non-console administrative access into the CDE.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | This requirement is intended to apply to<br>all<br>personnel<br>with administrative access to the CDE.<br>This requirement applies only to personnel<br>with<br>administrative access<br>and only for non-console<br>access to the CDE; it does not apply to<br>application<br>or<br>system accounts<br>performing automated functions.<br>If the entity does not use segmentation to separate<br>the CDE from the rest of their network, an<br>administrator could use<br>multi-factor authentication<br>either when logging<br>onto the CDE network or when<br>logging onto a system.<br>If the CDE is segmented from the rest of the entity's<br>network, an administrator would need to use multi<br>factor authentication<br>when<br>connecting to a CDE<br>system from a<br>non-CDE network.<br>Multi-factor<br>authentication<br>can<br>be implemented at network level<br>or at system/application level; it does not have to be<br>both. If the administrator uses MFA when logging<br>into the CDE network, they do not also need to use<br>MFA to log into a particular system or application<br>within the CDE. |
| administrative access.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | 8.3.1.b<br>Observe a sample of administrator personnel login to the<br>CDE and<br>verify that at least two of the three authentication<br>methods are used.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Incorporate multi-factor<br>8.3.2<br>authentication for<br>all<br>remote network<br>access<br>(both user and administrator, and<br>including third-party<br>access for support<br>or maintenance)<br>originating from outside<br>the<br>entity's<br>network. | 8.3.2.a Examine system configurations for remote access<br>servers and systems to verify multi-factor authentication is<br>required for:<br>•<br>All remote access by personnel, both user and<br>administrator, and<br>•<br>All third-party/vendor remote access (including access to<br>applications and system components for support or<br>maintenance purposes).<br>Observe a sample of personnel (for example, users and<br>8.3.2.b<br>administrators) connecting remotely to the network and verify<br>that at least two of the three authentication methods are used. | This requirement is intended to apply to all<br>personnel—including general users, administrators,<br>and vendors (for support or maintenance)<br>with<br>remote access to the network—where that remote<br>access could lead to access to the CDE. If remote<br>access is to an entity's network that has appropriate<br>segmentation, such that remote users cannot<br>access or impact the cardholder data environment,<br>multi-factor authentication for remote access to that<br>network would not be required. However, multi<br>factor authentication is required for any remote<br>access to networks with access to the cardholder<br>data environment, and is recommended for all<br>remote access to the entity's networks.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |

|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 8.4<br>Document and communicate<br>authentication policies<br>and procedures<br>to<br>all users including:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | 8.4.a Examine<br>procedures and interview personnel to verify that<br>authentication policies and procedures<br>are distributed to all<br>users.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Communicating password/authentication policies<br>and procedures to all users helps those users<br>understand and abide by the policies.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| •<br>Guidance on selecting strong<br>authentication credentials<br>•<br>Guidance for how users should<br>protect their authentication<br>credentials<br>•<br>Instructions not to reuse previously<br>used passwords<br>•<br>Instructions to<br>change passwords if<br>there is any suspicion the password<br>could be compromised.                                | 8.4.b Review authentication policies<br>and procedures<br>that are<br>distributed to users and verify they include:<br>•<br>Guidance on selecting strong authentication credentials<br>•<br>Guidance for how users should protect their authentication<br>credentials.<br>•<br>Instructions for users not to reuse previously used<br>passwords<br>•<br>Instructions to<br>change passwords if there is any suspicion<br>the password could be compromised.<br>8.4.c Interview a<br>sample of users to verify that they are familiar<br>with authentication policies<br>and procedures.                                                                                                                                 | For example, guidance on selecting strong<br>passwords may include suggestions to help<br>personnel select hard-to-guess passwords that don't<br>contain dictionary words, and that don't contain<br>information about the user (such as the user ID,<br>names of family members, date of birth, etc.).<br>Guidance for protecting authentication credentials<br>may include not writing down passwords or saving<br>them in insecure files, and being alert for malicious<br>individuals who may attempt to exploit their<br>passwords (for example, by calling an employee and<br>asking for their password so the caller can<br>"troubleshoot a problem").<br>Instructing users to change passwords if there is a<br>chance the password is no longer secure can<br>prevent malicious users from using a legitimate<br>password to gain unauthorized access. |
| Do not use group, shared, or generic<br>8.5<br>IDs, passwords, or other authentication<br>methods as follows:<br>•<br>Generic user IDs are disabled or<br>removed.<br>•<br>Shared user IDs do not exist for<br>system administration and other<br>critical functions.<br>•<br>Shared and generic user IDs are not<br>used to administer any system<br>components. | 8.5.a For a sample of system components, examine user ID lists<br>to verify the following:<br>•<br>Generic user IDs are disabled or removed.<br>•<br>Shared user IDs for system administration activities and other<br>critical functions do not exist.<br>•<br>Shared and generic user IDs are not used to administer any<br>system components.<br>8.5.b Examine authentication policies<br>and procedures to verify<br>that use of group and shared IDs and/or passwords or other<br>authentication methods are explicitly prohibited.<br>8.5.c<br>Interview system administrators to verify that group and<br>shared IDs and/or passwords or other authentication methods<br>are not distributed, even if requested. | If multiple<br>users share the same authentication<br>credentials (for example, user account and<br>password), it becomes impossible to trace system<br>access and activities to an individual. This in turn<br>prevents an entity from assigning accountability for,<br>or having effective logging of, an individual's actions,<br>since a given action could have been performed by<br>anyone in the group that has knowledge of the<br>authentication credentials.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |

| providers with remote access to<br>customer premises (for example, for<br>support of POS systems or servers)<br>must use a unique authentication<br>credential (such as a password/phrase)<br>for each customer. | procedures and interview personnel to verify that different<br>authentication credentials are used for access to each<br>customer.                                                                                      | To prevent the compromise of multiple customers<br>through the use of a single set of credentials,<br>vendors with remote access accounts to customer<br>environments should use a different authentication<br>credential for each customer. |

| accounts.<br>•<br>Physical and/or logical controls must<br>be in place to ensure<br>only the<br>intended account can use that                                                                                    | 8.6.b Interview security personnel to verify authentication<br>mechanisms are assigned to an account and not shared among<br>multiple accounts.                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| mechanism to gain access.                                                                                                                                                                                        | 8.6.c Examine system configuration settings and/or physical<br>controls, as applicable, to verify that controls are implemented to<br>ensure only the intended account can use that mechanism to<br>gain access.        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |

|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| All access to any database<br>8.7<br>containing cardholder data (including<br>access by applications, administrators,<br>and all other users) is restricted as<br>follows:<br>•<br>All user access to, user queries of,<br>and user actions on databases are<br>through programmatic methods.<br>•<br>Only database administrators have<br>the ability to directly access or query<br>databases.<br>•<br>Application IDs for database | 8.7.a Review database and application configuration settings<br>and verify that all users are authenticated prior to access.                                                                                                                                                         | Without user authentication for access to databases<br>and applications, the potential for unauthorized or<br>malicious access increases, and such access<br>cannot be logged since the user has not been<br>authenticated and is therefore not known to the<br>system. Also, database access should be granted<br>through programmatic methods only (for example,<br>through stored procedures), rather than via direct<br>access to the database by end users (except for<br>DBAs, who may need direct access to the database<br>for their administrative duties). |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | 8.7.b Examine database and application configuration settings to<br>verify that<br>all user access to, user queries of, and user actions on<br>(for example, move, copy, delete), the database are through<br>programmatic methods only (for example, through stored<br>procedures). |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | 8.7.c Examine database access control settings and database<br>application configuration settings to verify that user direct access<br>to or queries of databases are<br>restricted to database<br>administrators.                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| applications can only be used by the<br>applications (and not by individual<br>users or other non-application<br>processes).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | 8.7.d Examine database access control settings, database<br>application configuration settings, and the related application IDs<br>to verify that application IDs can only be used by the applications<br>(and not by individual users or other processes).                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 8.8<br>Ensure that security policies and<br>operational procedures for identification<br>and authentication are documented, in<br>use, and known to all affected parties.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Examine documentation<br>and<br>interview personnel to verify<br>8.8<br>that security policies and operational procedures for identification<br>and authentication are:<br>•<br>Documented,<br>•<br>In use, and<br>•<br>Known to all<br>affected parties.                            | Personnel need to be aware of and following<br>security policies and operational procedures for<br>managing identification and authorization on a<br>continuous basis.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

{78}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_78_Picture_0.jpeg)

#### <span id="page-78-0"></span>*Requirement 9: Restrict physical access to cardholder data*

Any physical access to data or systems that house cardholder data provides the opportunity for individuals to access devices or data and to remove systems or hardcopies, and should be appropriately restricted. For the purposes of Requirement 9, "onsite personnel" refers to full-time and part-time employees, temporary employees, contractors and consultants who are physically present on the entity's premises. A "visitor" refers to a vendor, guest of any onsite personnel, service workers, or anyone who needs to enter the facility for a short duration, usually not more than one day. "Media" refers to all paper and electronic media containing cardholder data.

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Use appropriate facility entry controls<br>9.1<br>to limit and monitor physical access to<br>systems in the cardholder data<br>environment.                                                                                                                                                          | Verify the existence of physical security controls for each<br>9.1<br>computer room, data center, and other physical areas with<br>systems in the cardholder data environment.<br>•<br>Verify that access is controlled with badge readers or other                  | Without physical access controls, such as badge<br>systems and door controls, unauthorized persons<br>could potentially gain access to the facility to steal,<br>disable, disrupt, or destroy critical systems and<br>cardholder data.                                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | devices including authorized badges and lock and key.<br>•<br>Observe a system administrator's attempt to log into consoles<br>for randomly selected systems in the cardholder data<br>environment and verify that they are "locked" to prevent<br>unauthorized use. | Locking console login<br>screens prevents<br>unauthorized persons<br>from<br>gaining access<br>to<br>sensitive information, altering system<br>configurations, introducing vulnerabilities into the<br>network, or destroying records.                                                                         |
| 9.1.1 Use either video cameras or<br>access control mechanisms (or both) to<br>monitor individual physical access to<br>sensitive areas. Review collected data                                                                                                                                       | 9.1.1.a Verify that either video cameras or access control<br>mechanisms (or both) are in place to monitor the entry/exit<br>points to sensitive areas.                                                                                                              | When investigating physical breaches, these<br>controls can help identify the individuals that<br>physically accessed the sensitive areas, as well as<br>when they entered and exited.                                                                                                                         |
| and correlate with other entries. Store<br>for at least three months, unless<br>otherwise restricted by law.                                                                                                                                                                                         | Verify that<br>either<br>video cameras or access control<br>9.1.1.b<br>mechanisms (or both) are protected<br>from tampering or disabling.                                                                                                                            | Criminals attempting to gain physical access to<br>sensitive areas will often attempt to disable or<br>bypass the monitoring controls. To protect these                                                                                                                                                        |
| "Sensitive areas" refers to any<br>Note:<br>data center, server room or any area that<br>houses systems that store, process, or<br>transmit cardholder data. This excludes<br>public-facing areas where only point-of<br>sale terminals are present, such as the<br>cashier areas in a retail store. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | controls from tampering, video cameras could be<br>positioned so they are out of reach and/or be<br>monitored to detect tampering. Similarly, access<br>control mechanisms could be monitored or have<br>physical protections installed to prevent them being<br>damaged or disabled by malicious individuals. |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | (Continued on next page)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |

|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | 9.1.1.c Verify that data from video cameras and/or access<br>control mechanisms is reviewed, and that data is stored for at<br>least three months.                                                       | Examples of sensitive areas include corporate<br>database server rooms, back-office rooms at retail<br>locations that store cardholder data, and storage<br>areas for large quantities of cardholder data.<br>Sensitive areas should be identified by each<br>organization to ensure the appropriate physical<br>monitoring controls are implemented.                                                                                                                                   |
| 9.1.2 Implement physical and/or logical<br>controls to restrict access to publicly<br>accessible network jacks.                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Interview responsible personnel and observe locations of<br>9.1.2<br>publicly<br>accessible network jacks to verify that physical and/or<br>logical controls are in place to restrict access to publicly | Restricting access to network jacks (or network<br>ports) will prevent malicious individuals from<br>plugging into readily available network jacks and                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| For example, network jacks located in<br>public areas and areas accessible to<br>visitors could be disabled and only<br>enabled when network access is<br>explicitly authorized. Alternatively,<br>processes could be implemented to<br>ensure that visitors are escorted at all<br>times in areas with active network jacks. | accessible network jacks.                                                                                                                                                                                | gain access<br>into internal network resources.<br>Whether logical or physical controls, or a<br>combination of both, are used, they should be<br>sufficient to prevent an individual or device that is<br>not explicitly authorized from being able to connect<br>to the network.                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 9.1.3 Restrict physical access to<br>wireless access points, gateways,<br>handheld devices,<br>networking/communications hardware,<br>and telecommunication lines.                                                                                                                                                            | 9.1.3 Verify that physical access to wireless access points,<br>gateways, handheld devices, networking/communications<br>hardware, and telecommunication lines is appropriately<br>restricted.           | Without security over access to wireless<br>components and devices, malicious users could<br>use an organization's unattended wireless devices<br>to access network resources, or even connect their<br>own devices to the wireless network to gain<br>unauthorized access. Additionally, securing<br>networking and communications hardware<br>prevents malicious users from intercepting network<br>traffic or physically connecting their own devices to<br>wired network resources. |

| Control physical access for onsite<br>9.3<br>personnel to sensitive areas as follows:<br>•<br>Access must be authorized and<br>based on individual job function.<br>•<br>Access is revoked immediately upon<br>termination, and all physical access<br>mechanisms, such as keys, access | For a sample of onsite personnel with physical access to<br>9.3.a<br>sensitive areas, interview responsible personnel<br>and observe<br>access control lists to verify that:<br>•<br>Access<br>to the sensitive area is authorized.<br>•<br>Access<br>is required for the individual's job function.<br>Observe personnel accessing<br>sensitive areas to verify that<br>9.3.b | Controlling physical access to sensitive areas<br>helps ensure that only authorized personnel with a<br>legitimate business need are granted access.<br>When personnel leave the organization, all<br>physical access mechanisms should be returned or<br>disabled promptly (as soon as possible) upon their<br>departure, to ensure personnel cannot gain |

| Procedures should include the following:                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | data).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |

| the visitors from<br>onsite personnel.                                                                                                            | Verify that visitor badges or other identification expire.<br>9.4.2.b                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Physically secure all media.<br>9.5                                                                                                                                                                                            | Verify that procedures for protecting cardholder data include<br>9.5<br>controls for physically securing all media (including but not limited<br>to computers, removable electronic media, paper receipts, paper<br>reports, and faxes).                                                                                                     | Controls for physically securing media<br>are intended<br>to prevent unauthorized persons from gaining<br>access to cardholder data on any type of media.<br>Cardholder data is susceptible to unauthorized<br>viewing, copying, or scanning if it is unprotected<br>while it is on removable or portable media, printed<br>out, or left on someone's desk.                                                                     |
| Store media backups<br>in a secure<br>9.5.1<br>location, preferably an off-site facility,<br>such as an alternate or backup<br>site, or<br>a commercial storage facility. Review<br>the location's security at least annually. | 9.5.1 Verify that the storage location security is reviewed at least<br>annually to confirm that backup media storage is secure.                                                                                                                                                                                                             | If stored in a non-secured facility, backups that<br>contain cardholder data may easily be lost, stolen,<br>or copied for malicious intent.<br>Periodically reviewing the storage facility enables<br>the organization to address identified security<br>issues in a timely manner, minimizing the potential<br>risk.                                                                                                           |
| Maintain strict control over the<br>9.6<br>internal or external distribution of any<br>kind of media, including the following:                                                                                                 | Verify that a policy exists to control distribution of media, and<br>9.6<br>that the policy covers all distributed media including that<br>distributed to individuals.                                                                                                                                                                       | Procedures and processes help protect cardholder<br>data on media distributed to internal and/or<br>external users. Without such procedures data can<br>be lost or stolen and used for fraudulent purposes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 9.6.1<br>Classify media so the sensitivity<br>of the data can be determined.                                                                                                                                                   | 9.6.1<br>Verify that all media is classified so the sensitivity of the<br>data can be determined.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | It is important that media be identified such that its<br>classification status can be easily discernible.<br>Media not identified as confidential may not be<br>adequately protected or may be lost or stolen.<br>This does not<br>mean the media needs to<br>Note:<br>have a "Confidential" label attached; the intent is<br>that the organization has identified media that<br>contains sensitive data so it can protect it. |
| Send the media by secured<br>9.6.2<br>courier or other delivery method that<br>can be accurately tracked.                                                                                                                      | Interview personnel and examine records to verify that<br>9.6.2.a<br>all media sent outside the facility is logged and sent via secured<br>courier or other delivery method that can be tracked.<br>Select a recent sample of several days of offsite tracking<br>9.6.2.b<br>logs for all media, and verify tracking details are documented. | Media may be lost or stolen if sent via a non<br>trackable method such as regular postal mail. Use<br>of secure couriers to deliver any media that<br>contains cardholder data allows organizations to<br>use their tracking systems to maintain inventory                                                                                                                                                                      |
