![](_page_83_Picture_0.jpeg)

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                           | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Ensure<br>management approves<br>9.6.3<br>any and all media that is moved from a<br>secured area (including when media is<br>distributed to individuals).      | Select a recent sample of several days of offsite tracking<br>9.6.3<br>logs for all media.<br>From examination of the logs and interviews<br>with responsible personnel, verify proper management<br>authorization is obtained whenever media is moved from a<br>secured area (including when media is distributed to<br>individuals).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Without a firm process for ensuring that all media<br>movements are approved before the media is<br>removed from secure areas, the media would not<br>be tracked or appropriately protected, and its<br>location would be unknown, leading to lost or<br>stolen media.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Maintain strict control over the<br>9.7<br>storage and accessibility of media.                                                                                 | Obtain and examine the policy for controlling storage and<br>9.7<br>maintenance of all media and verify that the policy requires<br>periodic media inventories.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Without careful inventory methods and storage<br>controls, stolen or missing media could go<br>unnoticed for an indefinite amount of time.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 9.7.1 Properly maintain inventory logs<br>of all media and conduct media<br>inventories at least annually.                                                     | Review media inventory logs to verify that logs are<br>9.7.1<br>maintained and media inventories are performed at least<br>annually.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | If media is not inventoried, stolen or lost media<br>may not be noticed for a long time or at all.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Destroy media when it is no longer<br>9.8<br>needed for business or legal reasons as<br>follows:                                                               | 9.8 Examine the periodic media destruction policy and verify that<br>it covers all media and defines requirements for the following:<br>•<br>Hard-copy materials must be crosscut shredded, incinerated,<br>or pulped such that there is reasonable assurance the hard<br>copy materials cannot be reconstructed.<br>•<br>Storage containers used for materials that are to be<br>destroyed must be secured.<br>•<br>Cardholder data on electronic media must be rendered<br>unrecoverable (e.g., via a secure wipe program in accordance<br>with industry-accepted standards for secure deletion, or by<br>physically destroying the media). | If steps are not taken to destroy information<br>contained on hard disks, portable drives,<br>CD/DVDs, or paper prior to disposal, malicious<br>individuals may be able to retrieve information from<br>the disposed media, leading to a data compromise.<br>For example, malicious individuals may use a<br>technique known as "dumpster diving," where they<br>search through trashcans and recycle bins looking<br>for information they can use to launch an attack.<br>Securing storage containers used for materials that<br>are going to be destroyed prevents sensitive<br>information from being captured while the materials<br>are being collected.<br>For example, "to-be-shredded"<br>containers could have a lock preventing access to<br>its contents or physic ally prevent access to the<br>inside of the container.<br>Examples of methods for securely destroying<br>electronic media include secure wiping,<br>degaussing, or physical destruction (such as<br>grinding or shredding hard disks). |
| Shred, incinerate, or pulp hard<br>9.8.1<br>copy materials so that cardholder data<br>cannot be reconstructed. Secure<br>storage containers used for materials | Interview personnel and examine procedures to verify<br>9.8.1.a<br>that hard-copy materials are crosscut shredded, incinerated, or<br>pulped such that there is reasonable assurance the hard-copy<br>materials cannot be reconstructed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| that are to be destroyed.                                                                                                                                      | Examine storage containers used for materials that<br>9.8.1.b<br>contain information to be destroyed to verify that the containers<br>are secured.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Render cardholder data on<br>9.8.2<br>electronic media unrecoverable so that<br>cardholder data cannot be<br>reconstructed.                                    | Verify that cardholder data on electronic media is rendered<br>9.8.2<br>unrecoverable (e.g., via a secure wipe program in accordance<br>with industry-accepted standards for secure deletion, or by<br>physically destroying the media).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 9.9 Protect devices that capture payment<br>card data via direct physical interaction<br>with the card from tampering and<br>substitution.<br>Note:<br>These requirements apply to card<br>reading devices used in card-present<br>transactions (that is, card swipe or dip) at<br>the point of sale. This requirement is not<br>intended to apply to manual key-entry<br>components such as computer<br>keyboards and POS keypads. | 9.9 Examine documented policies<br>and procedures to verify they<br>include:<br>•<br>Maintaining a list of devices<br>•<br>Periodically inspecting devices to look for tampering or<br>substitution<br>•<br>Training personnel to be aware of suspicious behavior<br>and<br>to report tampering or substitution of devices.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Criminals attempt to steal cardholder data by<br>stealing and/or manipulating card-reading devices<br>and terminals. For example, they will try to steal<br>devices so they can learn how to break into them,<br>and they often try to replace legitimate devices with<br>fraudulent devices that send them payment card<br>information every time a card is entered. Criminals<br>will also try to add "skimming" components to the<br>outside of devices, which are designed to capture<br>payment card details before they even enter the<br>device—for example, by attaching an additional<br>card reader on top of the legitimate card reader so<br>that the payment card details are captured twice:<br>once by the criminal's component and then by the<br>device's legitimate<br>component. In this way,<br>transactions may still be completed without<br>interruption while the criminal is "skimming"<br>the<br>payment card information during the process.<br>This requirement is recommended, but not<br>required, for manual key-entry components such<br>as computer keyboards and POS keypads.<br>Additional best practices on skimming prevention |
| Maintain an up-to-date list of<br>9.9.1<br>devices. The list should include the<br>following:<br>•<br>Make, model of device<br>•<br>Location of device (for example,<br>the address of the site or facility<br>where the device is located)<br>•<br>Device serial number or other<br>method of unique identification.                                                                                                               | Examine the list of devices to verify it includes:<br>9.9.1.a<br>•<br>Make, model of device<br>•<br>Location of device (for example, the address of the site or<br>facility where the device is located)<br>•<br>Device serial number or other method of unique<br>identification.<br>Select a sample of devices from the list and observe<br>9.9.1.b<br>devices and device<br>locations to verify that the list is accurate<br>and up<br>to<br>date.<br>Interview personnel to verify the list of devices is<br>9.9.1.c<br>updated when devices are added, relocated, decommissioned,<br>etc. | are available on the PCI SSC website.<br>Keeping an up-to-date list of devices helps an<br>organization keep track of where devices are<br>supposed to be, and quickly identify if a device is<br>missing or lost.<br>The method for maintaining a list of devices may<br>be automated (for example, a device-management<br>system) or manual (for example, documented in<br>electronic or paper records). For on-the-road<br>devices, the location may include the name of the<br>personnel to whom the device is assigned.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 9.9.2 Periodically inspect device<br>surfaces to detect tampering (for<br>example, addition of card skimmers to<br>devices), or substitution (for<br>example,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Examine documented procedures to verify processes<br>9.9.2.a<br>are defined to include the following:<br>•<br>Procedures for inspecting devices<br>•<br>Frequency of inspections.                                                                         | Regular inspections of devices will help<br>organizations to more quickly detect tampering or<br>replacement of<br>a device, and<br>thereby<br>minimize the<br>potential impact of using fraudulent devices.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| by checking the serial number or other<br>device characteristics to verify it has<br>not been swapped with a fraudulent<br>device).<br>Note:<br>Examples of signs that a device<br>might have been tampered with or<br>substituted include unexpected<br>attachments or cables plugged into the<br>device, missing or changed security<br>labels, broken or differently<br>colored<br>casing, or changes to the serial number<br>or other external markings. | Interview responsible personnel and observe inspection<br>9.9.2.b<br>processes to verify:<br>•<br>Personnel are aware of procedures for inspecting devices.<br>•<br>All devices are periodically inspected for evidence of<br>tampering and substitution. | The type of inspection will depend on the device—<br>for example, photographs of devices that are<br>known to be secure can be used to compare a<br>device's current appearance with its original<br>appearance to see whether<br>it has changed.<br>Another option may be to use a secure marker<br>pen, such as a UV light marker,<br>to mark device<br>surfaces and device openings so any tampering or<br>replacement<br>will be apparent. Criminals will often<br>replace the outer casing of a device to hide their<br>tampering, and these methods may help to detect<br>such activities. Device vendors may also be able to<br>provide security guidance and "how to" guides to<br>help determine whether<br>the device has been<br>tampered with.<br>The frequency of inspections will depend on factors<br>such as location of device and whether the device<br>is attended or unattended. For example, devices<br>left in public areas without supervision by the<br>organization's personnel may have more frequent |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | inspections than devices that<br>are kept in secure<br>areas or are supervised when they are accessible<br>to the public. The type and frequency of<br>inspections is determined by the merchant, as<br>defined by their annual risk-assessment process.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

| Provide training for personnel to<br>9.9.3<br>be aware of attempted tampering or<br>replacement of devices. verification<br>•<br>Being aware of suspicious behavior<br>around devices (for<br>example, attempts by unknown persons to unplug or open<br>devices)<br>•<br>Reporting suspicious behavior<br>and indications of device<br>tampering or<br>substitution to appropriate personnel (for<br>example, to a manager or security officer).<br>9.9.3.b<br>Interview a sample of personnel at point-of-sale<br>locations to verify they have received training and are aware of<br>the procedures for the following:<br>•<br>Verifying the identity of any third-party persons claiming to<br>be repair or maintenance personnel, prior to granting them<br>access to modify or troubleshoot devices<br>•<br>Not to install, replace,<br>or return devices without verification<br>•<br>Being aware of suspicious behavior<br>around devices (for<br>example, attempts by unknown persons to unplug or open<br>devices)<br>•<br>Reporting suspicious behavior<br>and indications of device<br>tampering or substitution to appropriate personnel (for<br>example, to a manager or security officer). | Criminals will often pose as authorized<br>maintenance personnel in order to gain access to<br>POS devices. All third parties requesting access to<br>devices should always be verified before being<br>provided access—for example, by checking with<br>management or phoning the POS maintenance<br>company (such as the vendor or acquirer) for<br>verification. Many criminals will try to fool personnel<br>by dressing for the part (for example, carrying<br>toolboxes and dressed in work wear), and could<br>also be knowledgeable about locations of devices,<br>so it's important personnel are trained to follow<br>procedures at all times.<br>Another trick criminals like to use is to send a<br>"new" POS system with instructions<br>for swapping it<br>with a legitimate system and "returning" the<br>legitimate system to a specified address. The<br>criminals may even provide return postage as they<br>are very keen to get their hands on these devices.<br>Personnel always verify with their manager or<br>supplier that the device is legitimate and came<br>from a trusted source before installing it or using it<br>for business. |
| 9.10<br>Ensure that security policies and<br>operational procedures for restricting<br>physical access to cardholder data are<br>documented, in use, and known to all<br>affected parties.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 9.10<br>Examine documentation<br>and<br>interview personnel to verify<br>that security policies and operational procedures for restricting<br>physical access to cardholder data are:<br>•<br>Documented,<br>•<br>In use, and<br>•<br>Known<br>to all affected parties.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Personnel need to be aware of and following<br>security policies and operational procedures for<br>restricting physical access to cardholder data and<br>CDE systems on a continuous basis.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

{87}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_87_Picture_0.jpeg)

### <span id="page-87-0"></span>**Regularly Monitor and Test Networks**

#### <span id="page-87-1"></span>*Requirement 10: Track and monitor all access to network resources and cardholder data*

Logging mechanisms and the ability to track user activities are critical in preventing, detecting, or minimizing the impact of a data compromise. The presence of logs in all environments allows thorough tracking, alerting, and analysis when something does go wrong. Determining the cause of a compromise is very difficult, if not impossible, without system activity logs.

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                       | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Implement audit trails to link all<br>10.1<br>access to system components to each<br>individual user.      | Verify,<br>through observation and interviewing the system<br>10.1<br>administrator, that:<br>•<br>Audit trails are enabled and active for system components.<br>•<br>Access to system components is linked to individual users. | It is critical to have a process or system that links<br>user access to system components accessed.<br>This system generates audit logs and provides the<br>ability to trace back suspicious activity to a<br>specific user.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| 10.2 Implement automated audit trails for<br>all system components to reconstruct the<br>following events: | Through interviews of responsible personnel, observation of<br>10.2<br>audit logs, and examination of audit log settings, perform the<br>following:                                                                              | Generating audit trails of suspect activities alerts<br>the system administrator, sends data to other<br>monitoring mechanisms (like intrusion detection<br>systems), and provides a history trail for post<br>incident follow-up. Logging of the following events<br>enables an organization to identify and trace<br>potentially malicious activities                                                  |
| All individual user accesses to<br>10.2.1<br>cardholder data                                               | Verify all individual access to cardholder data is logged.<br>10.2.1                                                                                                                                                             | Malicious individuals could obtain knowledge of a<br>user account with access to systems in the CDE,<br>or they could create a new, unauthorized account<br>in order to access cardholder data. A record of all<br>individual accesses to cardholder data can identify<br>which accounts may have been compromised or<br>misused.                                                                        |
| All actions taken by any<br>10.2.2<br>individual with root or administrative<br>privileges                 | Verify all actions taken by any individual with root or<br>10.2.2<br>administrative privileges are logged.                                                                                                                       | Accounts with increased privileges, such as the<br>"administrator" or "root" account, have the<br>potential to greatly impact the security or<br>operational functionality of a system. Without a log<br>of the activities performed, an organization is<br>unable to trace any issues resulting from an<br>administrative mistake or misuse of privilege back<br>to the specific action and individual. |

{88}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_88_Picture_0.jpeg)

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                         | Testing Procedures                                                                                                               | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 10.2.3<br>Access to all audit trails                                                                         | 10.2.3<br>Verify access to all audit trails is logged.                                                                           | Malicious users often attempt to alter audit logs to<br>hide their actions, and a record of access allows<br>an organization to trace any inconsistencies or<br>potential tampering of the logs to an individual<br>account. Having access to logs identifying<br>changes, additions,<br>and deletions can help retrace<br>steps made by unauthorized personnel.                            |
| Invalid logical access attempts<br>10.2.4                                                                    | Verify invalid logical access attempts are logged.<br>10.2.4                                                                     | Malicious individuals will often perform multiple<br>access attempts on targeted systems. Multiple<br>invalid login attempts may be an indication of an<br>unauthorized user's attempts to "brute force" or<br>guess a password.                                                                                                                                                            |
| Use of and changes to<br>10.2.5<br>identification and authentication<br>mechanisms—including but not limited | Verify use of identification and authentication<br>10.2.5.a<br>mechanisms is logged.                                             | Without knowing who was logged on at the time of<br>an incident, it is impossible to identify the<br>accounts that<br>may have been<br>used. Additionally,<br>malicious users may attempt to manipulate the<br>authentication controls with the intent of                                                                                                                                   |
| to creation of new accounts<br>and<br>elevation of privileges—and all                                        | 10.2.5.b<br>Verify all elevation of privileges is logged.                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| changes, additions, or deletions to<br>accounts with root or administrative<br>privileges                    | Verify all changes, additions, or deletions to any account<br>10.2.5.c<br>with root or administrative privileges are logged.     | bypassing them or impersonating a valid account.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| Initialization, stopping,<br>or<br>10.2.6<br>pausing of the audit logs                                       | Verify the following are logged:<br>10.2.6<br>•<br>Initialization<br>of audit logs<br>•<br>Stopping<br>or pausing of audit logs. | Turning the audit logs off (or pausing them) prior<br>to performing illicit activities is a common practice<br>for malicious users wishing to avoid detection.<br>Initialization of audit logs could indicate that the<br>log function was disabled by a user to hide their<br>actions.                                                                                                     |
| Creation and deletion of system<br>10.2.7<br>level objects                                                   | Verify creation and deletion of system level objects are<br>10.2.7<br>logged.                                                    | Malicious software, such as malware, often<br>creates or replaces system level objects on the<br>target system in order to control a particular<br>function or operation on that system. By logging<br>when system-level objects, such as database<br>tables or stored procedures, are created or<br>deleted, it will be easier to determine whether<br>such modifications were authorized. |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                 | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                       | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Record at least the following audit<br>10.3<br>trail entries for all system components for<br>each event:                                                                                            | Through interviews and observation of audit logs, for each<br>10.3<br>auditable event (from 10.2), perform the following:                                                                | By recording these details for the auditable events<br>at 10.2, a potential compromise can be quickly<br>identified, and with sufficient detail to know who,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| User identification<br>10.3.1                                                                                                                                                                        | Verify user identification is included in log entries.<br>10.3.1                                                                                                                         | what, where, when, and how.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Type of event<br>10.3.2                                                                                                                                                                              | 10.3.2 Verify type of event is included in log entries.                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Date and time<br>10.3.3                                                                                                                                                                              | 10.3.3 Verify date and time stamp is included in log entries.                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| 10.3.4 Success or failure indication                                                                                                                                                                 | 10.3.4 Verify success or failure indication is included in log<br>entries.                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Origination of event<br>10.3.5                                                                                                                                                                       | 10.3.5 Verify origination of event is included in log entries.                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Identity or name of affected<br>10.3.6<br>data, system component,<br>or resource.                                                                                                                    | 10.3.6 Verify identity or name of affected data, system<br>component, or resources is included in log entries.                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| 10.4 Using time-synchronization<br>technology, synchronize all critical<br>system clocks and times and ensure that<br>the following is implemented for<br>acquiring, distributing, and storing time. | 10.4 Examine configuration standards and processes to verify that<br>time-synchronization technology is implemented<br>and kept current<br>per PCI DSS Requirements 6.1 and 6.2.         | Time synchronization technology is used to<br>synchronize clocks on multiple systems. When<br>clocks are not properly synchronized, it can be<br>difficult,<br>if not impossible,<br>to compare log files<br>from different systems and establish an exact<br>sequence of event (crucial for forensic analysis in<br>the event of a breach). For post-incident forensics<br>teams, the accuracy and consistency of time<br>across all systems and the time of each activity is<br>critical in determining how the systems were<br>compromised. |
| Note: One example of time<br>synchronization technology is Network<br>Time Protocol (NTP).                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Critical systems have the<br>10.4.1<br>correct and consistent time.                                                                                                                                  | Examine the process for acquiring, distributing and<br>10.4.1.a<br>storing the correct time within the organization to verify that:                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                      | •<br>Only the designated central time server(s) receives time<br>signals from external sources, and time signals from external<br>sources are based on International Atomic Time or UTC. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                      | •<br>Where there is more than one designated time server, the<br>time servers peer with one another<br>to keep accurate time,                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                      | •<br>Systems receive time information only from designated<br>central time server(s).                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|                                                                           | 10.4.1.b<br>Observe the time-related system-parameter settings for<br>a sample of system components to verify:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                           | •<br>Only the designated central time server(s) receives time<br>signals from external sources, and time signals from external<br>sources are based on International Atomic Time or UTC.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                           | •<br>Where there is more than one designated time server, the<br>designated central time server(s) peer with one another<br>to<br>keep accurate time.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                           | •<br>Systems receive time only from designated central time<br>server(s).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Time data is protected.<br>10.4.2                                         | Examine system configurations and time<br>10.4.2.a<br>synchronization settings to verify that access to time data is<br>restricted to only personnel with a business need to access time<br>data.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|                                                                           | 10.4.2.b Examine system configurations, time synchronization<br>settings and logs, and processes to verify that any changes to<br>time settings on critical systems are logged, monitored, and<br>reviewed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 10.4.3 Time settings are received from<br>industry-accepted time sources. | 10.4.3 Examine systems configurations to verify that the time<br>server(s) accept time updates from specific, industry-accepted<br>external sources (to prevent a malicious individual from changing<br>the clock). Optionally, those updates can be encrypted with a<br>symmetric key, and access control lists can be created that<br>specify the IP addresses of client machines that will be provided<br>with the time updates (to prevent unauthorized use of internal<br>time servers). |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Secure audit trails so they cannot<br>10.5<br>be altered.                 | Interview system administrators and examine system<br>10.5<br>configurations and permissions to verify that audit trails are<br>secured so that they cannot be altered as follows:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Often a malicious individual who has entered the<br>network will attempt to edit the audit logs in order<br>to hide their activity. Without adequate protection<br>of audit logs, their completeness, accuracy, and<br>integrity cannot be guaranteed, and the audit logs<br>can be rendered useless as an investigation tool<br>after a compromise. |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                 | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 10.5.1 Limit viewing of audit trails to<br>those with a job-related need.                                                                                                                                                        | 10.5.1 Only individuals who have a job-related need can view<br>audit trail files.                                                                                                 | Adequate protection of the audit logs includes<br>strong access control (limit access to logs based<br>on "need to know" only), and use of<br>physical or<br>network segregation to make the logs harder to<br>find and modify.                                                                             |
| 10.5.2 Protect audit trail files from<br>unauthorized modifications.                                                                                                                                                             | 10.5.2 Current audit trail files are protected from unauthorized<br>modifications via access control mechanisms, physical<br>segregation, and/or network segregation.              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| 10.5.3 Promptly back up audit trail files<br>to a centralized log server or media<br>that is difficult to alter.                                                                                                                 | 10.5.3 Current audit trail files are promptly backed up to a<br>centralized log server or media that is difficult to alter.                                                        | Promptly backing up the logs to a centralized log<br>server or media that is<br>difficult to alter keeps the<br>logs protected even if the system generating the<br>logs becomes compromised.                                                                                                               |
| 10.5.4 Write logs for external-facing<br>technologies onto a secure,<br>centralized, internal log server or<br>media device.                                                                                                     | 10.5.4 Logs for external-facing technologies (for example,<br>wireless, firewalls, DNS, mail) are written onto a secure,<br>centralized, internal log server or media.             | By writing logs from external-facing technologies<br>such as wireless, firewalls, DNS, and mail<br>servers, the risk of those logs being lost or altered<br>is lowered, as they are more secure within the<br>internal network.                                                                             |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                    | Logs may be written directly, or offloaded or<br>copied from external systems,<br>to the secure<br>internal system or media.                                                                                                                                                                                |
| 10.5.5 Use file-integrity monitoring or<br>change-detection software on logs to<br>ensure that existing log data cannot be<br>changed without generating alerts<br>(although new data being added<br>should not cause an alert). | 10.5.5 Examine system settings, monitored files, and results from<br>monitoring activities to verify the use of file-integrity monitoring or<br>change-detection software on logs. | File-integrity monitoring or change-detection<br>systems check for changes to critical<br>files, and<br>notify when such changes are noted. For file<br>integrity monitoring purposes, an entity usually<br>monitors files that don't regularly change, but<br>when changed indicate a possible compromise. |
| Review logs and security events for<br>10.6<br>all system components to identify<br>anomalies or suspicious activity.                                                                                                            | Perform the following:<br>10.6                                                                                                                                                     | Many breaches occur over days or months before<br>being detected. Regular log reviews by personnel<br>or automated means can identify and proactively                                                                                                                                                       |
| Log harvesting, parsing, and<br>Note:                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                    | address unauthorized access to the cardholder<br>data environment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| alerting tools may be used to meet this<br>Requirement.                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                    | The log review process does not have to be<br>manual. The use of log harvesting, parsing, and<br>alerting tools can help facilitate the process by<br>identifying log events that need to be reviewed.                                                                                                      |

| PCI DSS Requirements
