                                                                                                          |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| <ul> <li>10.6.1 Review the following at least daily:</li> <li>All security events</li> <li>Logs of all system components that store, process, or transmit CHD and/or SAD</li> <li>Logs of all critical system components</li> <li>Logs of all servers and system components that perform security functions (for example, firewalls, intrusion-detection systems/intrusion-prevention systems (IDS/IPS), authentication servers, e-commerce redirection servers, etc.).</li> </ul> | <ul> <li>10.6.1.a Examine security policies and procedures to verify that procedures are defined for reviewing the following at least daily, either manually or via log tools: <ul> <li>All security events</li> <li>Logs of all system components that store, process, or transmit CHD and/or SAD</li> <li>Logs of all critical system components</li> <li>Logs of all servers and system components that perform security functions (for example, firewalls, intrusion-detection systems/intrusion-prevention systems (IDS/IPS), authentication servers, e-commerce redirection servers, etc.)</li> </ul> </li> <li>10.6.1.b Observe processes and interview personnel to verify that the following are reviewed at least daily: <ul> <li>All security events</li> <li>Logs of all system components that store, process, or transmit CHD and/or SAD</li> <li>Logs of all critical system components</li> <li>Logs of all servers and system components that perform security functions (for example, firewalls, intrusion-detection systems/intrusion-prevention systems (IDS/IPS), authentication servers, e-commerce redirection servers, etc.).</li> </ul> </li></ul> | Checking logs daily minimizes the amount of time and exposure of a potential breach.  Daily review of security events—for example, notifications or alerts that identify suspicious or anomalous activities—as well as logs from critical system components, and logs from systems that perform security functions, such as firewalls, IDS/IPS, file-integrity monitoring (FIM) systems, etc. is necessary to identify potential issues. Note that the determination of "security event" will vary for each organization and may include consideration for the type of technology, location, and function of the device. Organizations may also wish to maintain a baseline of "normal" traffic to help identify anomalous behavior. |
| 10.6.2 Review logs of all other system components periodically based on the organization's policies and risk management strategy, as determined by the organization's annual risk                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 10.6.2.a Examine security policies and procedures to verify that procedures are defined for reviewing logs of all other system components periodically—either manually or via log tools—based on the organization's policies and risk management strategy.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Logs for all other system components should also be periodically reviewed to identify indications of potential issues or attempts to gain access to sensitive systems via less-sensitive systems. The frequency of the reviews should be determined by an entity's annual risk assessment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| assessment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | <b>10.6.2.b</b> Examine the organization's risk-assessment documentation and interview personnel to verify that reviews are performed in accordance with organization's policies and risk management strategy.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | | <b>10.6.3</b> Follow up exceptions and anomalies identified during the review process.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | <b>10.6.3.a</b> Examine security policies and procedures to verify that procedures are defined for following up on exceptions and anomalies identified during the review process.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | If exceptions and anomalies identified during the log-review process are not investigated, the entity may be unaware of unauthorized and potentially                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| malicious activities that are occurring within their own network. | PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Retain audit trail history for at least<br>10.7<br>one year, with a minimum of three<br>months immediately available for<br>analysis (for example, online, archived,<br>or restorable from backup).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Examine security policies and procedures to verify that they<br>10.7.a<br>define the<br>following:<br>•<br>Audit log retention policies<br>•<br>Procedures for retaining audit logs for at least one year, with<br>a minimum of three months immediately available online.<br>Interview personnel and examine audit logs to verify that<br>10.7.b<br>audit logs are retained for at least one year.<br>Interview personnel and observe processes to verify that at<br>10.7.c<br>least the last three months' logs are immediately available for<br>analysis.                                                                                                                                                        | Retaining logs for at least a year allows for the<br>fact that it often takes a while to notice that a<br>compromise has occurred or is occurring, and<br>allows investigators sufficient log history to better<br>determine the length of time of a potential breach<br>and potential system(s) impacted. By having three<br>months of logs immediately available, an entity<br>can quickly identify and minimize impact of a data<br>breach. Storing logs in off-line locations could<br>prevent them from being readily available,<br>resulting in longer time frames to restore log data,<br>perform analysis, and identify impacted systems<br>or data.                              |
| 10.8<br>Additional requirement for<br>service providers only: Implement a<br>process for the timely detection and<br>reporting of failures of critical security<br>control systems, including<br>but not limited<br>to failure of:<br>•<br>Firewalls<br>•<br>IDS/IPS<br>•<br>FIM<br>•<br>Anti-virus<br>•<br>Physical access controls<br>•<br>Logical access controls<br>•<br>Audit logging mechanisms<br>•<br>Segmentation controls (if used) | Examine documented policies and procedures to verify that<br>10.8.a<br>processes are defined<br>for the timely detection and reporting<br>of<br>failures of critical security control systems, including<br>but not limited<br>to failure of:<br>•<br>Firewalls<br>•<br>IDS/IPS<br>•<br>FIM<br>•<br>Anti-virus<br>•<br>Physical access controls<br>•<br>Logical access controls<br>•<br>Audit logging mechanisms<br>•<br>Segmentation controls (if used)<br>Examine detection and alerting processes and interview<br>10.8.b<br>personnel to verify that processes are implemented for all critical<br>security controls, and that failure of a critical security control results<br>in the generation of an alert. | Note: This requirement applies only when the<br>entity being assessed is a service provider.<br>Without formal processes to detect and alert when<br>critical security controls<br>fail, failures may go<br>undetected for extended periods and provide<br>attackers ample time to compromise systems and<br>steal sensitive data from the cardholder data<br>environment.<br>The specific types of failures may vary depending<br>on the function of the device and technology in<br>use. Typical failures include a system ceasing to<br>perform its security function or not functioning in<br>its intended manner; for example, a firewall<br>erasing all its rules or going offline. |

(date and time start and end) of the security failure<br>•<br>Details of the remediation required to address the root<br>cause | Note: This requirement applies only when the<br>entity being assessed is a service provider.<br>If critical security control failures<br>alerts<br>are not<br>quickly and effectively responded<br>to, attackers<br>may use this time to insert malicious software,<br>gain control of a system,<br>or steal data from the<br>entity's environment.<br>Documented evidence (e.g., records within a<br>problem management system) should support that<br>processes and procedures are in place to respond<br>to security failures. ![](_page_95_Picture_0.jpeg)

#### <span id="page-95-0"></span>*Requirement 11: Regularly test security systems and processes.*

Vulnerabilities are being discovered continually by malicious individuals and researchers, and being introduced by new software. System components, processes, and custom software should be tested frequently to ensure security controls continue to reflect a changing environment.

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Testing<br>Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 11.1<br>Implement processes to test for the<br>presence of wireless access points<br>(802.11), and detect and identify all<br>authorized and unauthorized wireless<br>access points on a quarterly basis.<br>Methods that may be used in the<br>Note:<br>process include but are not limited to<br>wireless network scans, physical/logical<br>inspections of system components and<br>infrastructure, network access control<br>(NAC), or wireless IDS/IPS.<br>Whichever methods are used, they must be<br>sufficient to detect and identify both<br>authorized and unauthorized devices. | 11.1.a Examine policies and procedures to verify processes<br>are defined for detection<br>and identification of both authorized<br>and unauthorized wireless access points on a quarterly<br>basis.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Implementation and/or exploitation of wireless<br>technology within a network are some of the most<br>common paths for malicious users to gain access<br>to the network and cardholder data. If a wireless                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | 11.1.b Verify that the methodology is adequate to detect and<br>identify any unauthorized wireless access points, including at<br>least the following:<br>•<br>WLAN cards inserted into system components<br>•<br>Portable or mobile devices attached to system<br>components to create a wireless access point (for<br>example, by USB, etc.)<br>•<br>Wireless devices attached to a network port or network<br>device.<br>11.1.c If wireless scanning is utilized, examine output from<br>recent wireless scans to verify that:<br>•<br>Authorized<br>and unauthorized wireless access points are | device or network is installed without a company's<br>knowledge, it can allow an attacker to easily and<br>"invisibly" enter the network. Unauthorized wireless<br>devices may be hidden within or attached to a<br>computer or other system component, or be<br>attached directly to a network port or network<br>device, such as a switch or router. Any such<br>unauthorized device could result in an<br>unauthorized access point into the environment.<br>Knowing which wireless devices are authorized<br>can help administrators quickly identify non<br>authorized wireless devices, and responding to the<br>identification of unauthorized wireless access<br>points helps to proactively minimize the exposure |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | identified, and<br>•<br>The<br>scan is performed at least quarterly for all system<br>components and facilities.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | of CDE to malicious individuals.<br>Due to the ease with which a wireless access point<br>can be attached to a network, the difficulty in<br>detecting their presence, and the increased risk                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | 11.1.d If automated monitoring is utilized (for example,<br>wireless IDS/IPS, NAC, etc.), verify the configuration will<br>generate alerts to notify personnel.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | presented by unauthorized wireless devices, these<br>processes must be performed even when a policy<br>exists prohibiting the use of wireless technology.<br>The size and complexity of a particular<br>environment will dictate the appropriate tools<br>and<br>processes to be used to provide sufficient                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | assurance that a rogue wireless access point has<br>not been installed in the environment.<br>(Continued<br>on next page)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |

{96}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_96_Picture_0.jpeg)

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                      | Testing<br>Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                              | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 11.1.1 Maintain an inventory of<br>authorized wireless access points<br>including a documented business<br>justification. | 11.1.1 Examine documented records to verify that an<br>inventory of authorized wireless access points is maintained<br>and a business justification is documented for<br>all authorized<br>wireless access points. | In the case of a single standalone<br>For example:<br>retail kiosk in a shopping mall, where all<br>communication components are contained within<br>tamper-resistant and tamper-evident casings,<br>performing a detailed physical inspection of the<br>kiosk itself may be sufficient to provide assurance<br>that a rogue wireless access point has not been<br>attached or installed. However, in an environment<br>with multiple nodes (such as in a large retail store,<br>call center, server room or data center), detailed<br>physical inspection is difficult. In this case, multiple<br>methods may be combined to meet the<br>requirement, such as performing physical system<br>inspections in conjunction with the results of a<br>wireless analyzer. |
| 11.1.2 Implement incident response<br>procedures in the event unauthorized<br>wireless access points are detected.        | 11.1.2.a<br>Examine the organization's incident response plan<br>(Requirement 12.10) to verify it defines and requires a<br>response in the event that an unauthorized wireless access<br>point is detected.       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                           | Interview responsible personnel and/or inspect<br>11.1.2.b<br>recent wireless scans and related responses to verify action<br>is taken when unauthorized wireless access points are<br>found.                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |

| 11.3 Implement a methodology for<br>penetration testing that includes the<br>following:<br>•<br>Is based<br>on industry-accepted<br>penetration testing approaches (for<br>example,<br>NIST SP800-115)<br>•<br>Includes coverage<br>for the entire CDE<br>perimeter and critical systems<br>•<br>Includes testing<br>from both inside and<br>outside the network<br>•<br>Includes testing<br>to validate any<br>segmentation and scope-reduction<br>controls<br>•<br>Defines application-layer penetration<br>tests to include, at a minimum, the<br>vulnerabilities listed in Requirement 6.5<br>•<br>Defines network-layer penetration tests<br>to include components that support<br>network functions as well as operating<br>systems<br>•<br>Includes review<br>and consideration of<br>threats and vulnerabilities experienced<br>in the last 12 months<br>•<br>Specifies retention<br>of penetration<br>testing results and remediation<br>activities results. | Examine penetration-testing methodology and interview<br>11.3<br>responsible personnel to verify a methodology is implemented<br>that includes the following:<br>•<br>Is based<br>on industry-accepted penetration testing<br>approaches<br>(for example,<br>NIST SP800-115)<br>•<br>Includes coverage<br>for the entire CDE perimeter and<br>critical systems<br>•<br>Testing from both inside and outside the network<br>•<br>Includes testing<br>to validate any segmentation and scope<br>reduction controls<br>•<br>Defines application-layer penetration tests to include, at a<br>minimum, the vulnerabilities listed in Requirement 6.5<br>•<br>Defines network-layer penetration tests to include<br>components that support network functions as well as<br>operating systems<br>•<br>Includes review<br>and consideration of threats and<br>vulnerabilities experienced in the last 12 months<br>•<br>Specifies retention<br>of penetration testing results and<br>remediation activities results. | The intent of a penetration test is to simulate a<br>real-world attack situation with a goal of identifying<br>how far an attacker would be able to penetrate into<br>an environment. This allows an entity to gain a<br>better understanding of their potential exposure<br>and develop a strategy to defend against attacks.<br>A penetration test differs from a vulnerability scan,<br>as a penetration test is an active process that<br>may<br>include exploiting identified vulnerabilities.<br>Conducting<br>a vulnerability scan may be one of the<br>first steps a penetration tester will perform in order<br>to plan the testing strategy, although it is not the<br>only step. Even if a vulnerability scan does not<br>detect known vulnerabilities, the penetration tester<br>will often gain enough knowledge about the system<br>to identify possible security gaps.<br>Penetration testing is generally a highly manual<br>process. While some automated tools may be<br>used, the tester uses their knowledge of systems<br>to penetrate<br>into an environment. Often the tester<br>will chain several types of exploits together with a<br>goal of breaking through layers of defenses. For<br>example, if the tester finds a means to gain access<br>to an application server, they will then use the<br>compromised server as a point to stage a new<br>attack based on the resources the server has<br>access to. In this way, a tester is able to simulate<br>the methods performed by an attacker to identify<br>areas of potential weakness in the environment.<br>Penetration<br>testing techniques will be different for<br>different organizations, and the type, depth, and<br>complexity of the testing will depend on the specific<br>environment and the organization's risk<br>assessment. |

|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | 11.3.1.b Verify that the test was performed by a qualified<br>internal resource or qualified external third party and,<br>if<br>applicable, organizational independence of the tester exists<br>(not required to be a QSA or ASV).                                                               | configuration of a given environment. If an upgrade<br>or modification could allow access to cardholder<br>data or affect the security of the cardholder data<br>environment, then it could be considered                                                                                                                            |
| 11.3.2<br>Perform internal<br>penetration<br>testing at least annually and after any<br>significant infrastructure or application<br>upgrade or modification (such as an<br>operating system upgrade, a sub-network<br>added to the environment, or a web<br>server added to the environment). | 11.3.2.a<br>Examine the scope of work and results from the<br>most recent internal penetration test to verify that<br>penetration testing is performed<br>as follows.<br>•<br>Per the defined methodology<br>•<br>At least annually<br>•<br>After any significant changes to the environment.    | significant. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | 11.3.2.b Verify that the test was performed by a qualified<br>internal resource or qualified external third party and,<br>if<br>applicable, organizational independence of the tester exists<br>(not required to be a QSA or ASV).                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 11.3.3 Exploitable vulnerabilities found<br>during penetration testing are corrected<br>and testing is repeated to verify the<br>corrections.                                                                                                                                                  | 11.3.3 Examine penetration testing results to verify that<br>noted exploitable vulnerabilities were corrected and that<br>repeated testing confirmed the vulnerability was corrected.                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Testing<br>Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Guidance                                                                                                                                                     |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| For change-detection purposes,<br>Note:<br>critical files are usually those that do not<br>regularly change, but the modification of<br>which could indicate a system compromise<br>or risk of compromise. Change-detection<br>mechanisms such as file-integrity<br>monitoring products usually come pre<br>configured with critical files for the related<br>operating system. Other critical files, such<br>as those for custom applications, must be<br>evaluated and defined by the entity (that is,<br>the merchant or service provider). | Verify the mechanism is configured to alert personnel<br>11.5.b<br>to unauthorized modification (including changes, additions,<br>and deletions)<br>of critical files, and to perform critical file<br>comparisons at least weekly.                   |                                                                                                                                                              |

{104}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_104_Picture_0.jpeg)

### <span id="page-104-0"></span>**Maintain an Information Security Policy**

#### <span id="page-104-1"></span>*Requirement 12: Maintain a policy that addresses information security for all personnel.*

A strong security policy sets the security tone for the whole entity and informs personnel what is expected of them. All personnel should be aware of the sensitivity of data and their responsibilities for protecting it. For the purposes of Requirement 12, "personnel" refers to full-time and part-time employees, temporary employees, contractors and consultants who are "resident" on the entity's site or otherwise have access to the cardholder data environment.

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 12.1 Establish, publish, maintain, and<br>disseminate a security policy.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Examine the information security policy and verify that<br>12.1<br>the policy is published and disseminated to all relevant<br>personnel<br>(including vendors and business partners).                                                                                                                                                                                                      | A company's information security policy creates<br>the roadmap for implementing security measures<br>to protect its most valuable assets. All personnel<br>should be aware of the sensitivity of data and their<br>responsibilities for protecting it.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Review the security policy at least<br>12.1.1<br>annually and update the policy<br>when the<br>environment changes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | 12.1.1 Verify that the information security policy is<br>reviewed at least annually and updated as needed to<br>reflect changes to business objectives or the risk<br>environment.                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Security threats and protection methods evolve<br>rapidly. Without updating the security policy to<br>reflect relevant changes, new protection measures<br>to fight against these threats are not addressed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 12.2 Implement a risk-assessment process<br>that:<br>•<br>Is<br>performed at least annually and upon<br>significant changes to the environment<br>(for example, acquisition, merger,<br>relocation, etc.),<br>•<br>Identifies critical assets, threats,<br>and<br>vulnerabilities, and<br>•<br>Results in a formal, documented<br>analysis<br>of risk.<br>Examples of risk-assessment methodologies<br>include but are not limited to OCTAVE, ISO<br>27005 and NIST SP 800-30. | 12.2.a Verify that an annual risk-assessment process is<br>documented that:<br>•<br>Identifies critical assets, threats,<br>and vulnerabilities<br>•<br>Results in a formal, documented<br>analysis of risk<br>12.2.b Review risk-assessment documentation to verify<br>that the risk-assessment process is performed at least<br>annually and upon significant changes to the environment. | A risk assessment enables an organization to<br>identify threats and associated vulnerabilities with<br>the potential to negatively impact their business.<br>Examples of different risk considerations include<br>cybercrime, web attacks, and POS malware.<br>Resources can then be effectively allocated to<br>implement controls that reduce the likelihood<br>and/or the potential impact of the threat being<br>realized.<br>Performing risk assessments at least annually and<br>upon significant changes allows the organization<br>to keep up to date with organizational changes<br>and evolving threats, trends,<br>and technologies. |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                         | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                       | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 12.3 Develop usage policies for critical<br>technologies and define proper use of these<br>technologies.                                                                                                     | 12.3 Examine the usage policies for critical technologies<br>and interview responsible personnel to verify the following<br>policies are implemented and followed:                       | Personnel usage policies can either prohibit use of<br>certain devices and other technologies if that is<br>company policy, or provide guidance for personnel                                                                                                                                             |
| Note: Examples of critical technologies<br>include, but are not limited to, remote access<br>and wireless technologies, laptops, tablets,<br>removable electronic media, e-mail usage and<br>Internet usage. |                                                                                                                                                                                          | as to correct usage and implementation. If usage<br>policies are not in place, personnel may use the<br>technologies in violation of company policy,<br>thereby allowing malicious individuals to gain<br>access to critical systems and cardholder data.                                                 |
| Ensure these usage policies require the<br>following:                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Explicit approval by authorized parties<br>12.3.1                                                                                                                                                            | 12.3.1 Verify that the usage policies include processes<br>for explicit approval from authorized parties to use the<br>technologies.                                                     | Without requiring proper approval for<br>implementation of these technologies, individual<br>personnel may innocently implement a solution to<br>a perceived business need, but also open a huge<br>hole that subjects critical systems and data to<br>malicious individuals.                             |
| 12.3.2 Authentication for use of the<br>technology                                                                                                                                                           | 12.3.2 Verify that the usage policies include processes<br>for all technology use to be authenticated with user ID<br>and password or other authentication item (for example,<br>token). | If technology is implemented without proper<br>authentication (user IDs and passwords, tokens,<br>VPNs, etc.), malicious individuals may easily use<br>this unprotected technology to access critical<br>systems and cardholder data.                                                                     |
| 12.3.3 A list of all such<br>devices and<br>personnel with access                                                                                                                                            | 12.3.3 Verify that the usage policies define:<br>•<br>A<br>list of all critical devices,<br>and<br>•<br>A list of personnel authorized to use the devices.                               | Malicious individuals may breach physical security<br>and place their own devices on the network as a<br>"back door." Personnel may also bypass<br>procedures and install devices. An accurate<br>inventory with proper device labeling allows for<br>quick identification of non-approved installations. |

|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

Logical labeling<br>may be employed with information such as codes<br>that can correlate the device to its owner, contact<br>information,<br>and purpose. |
| 12.3.5 Acceptable uses of the technology                                                                                                                                               | 12.3.5 Verify that the usage policies define acceptable<br>uses for the technology.                                                                                                                                             | By defining acceptable business use and location<br>of company-approved devices and technology, the                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| 12.3.6 Acceptable network locations for the<br>technologies                                                                                                                            | 12.3.6 Verify that the usage policies define acceptable<br>network locations for the technology.                                                                                                                                | company is better able to manage and control<br>gaps in configurations and operational controls, to<br>ensure a "back door" is not opened for a malicious                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| 12.3.7 List of company-approved products                                                                                                                                               | 12.3.7 Verify that the usage policies include a list of<br>company-approved products.                                                                                                                                           | individual to gain access to critical systems and<br>cardholder data.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| 12.3.8 Automatic disconnect of sessions for<br>remote-access technologies after a specific<br>period of inactivity                                                                     | 12.3.8.a Verify that the usage policies require automatic<br>disconnect of sessions for remote-access technologies<br>after a specific period of inactivity.                                                                    | Remote-access technologies are frequent "back<br>doors" to critical resources and cardholder data.<br>By disconnecting remote-access technologies<br>when not in use (for example, those used to<br>support your systems by your POS vendor, other<br>vendors, or business partners), access and risk to<br>networks is minimized.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                                                                        | Examine configurations for remote access<br>12.3.8.b<br>technologies to verify that remote access sessions will be<br>automatically disconnected after a specific period of<br>inactivity.                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| 12.3.9 Activation of remote-access<br>technologies for vendors and business<br>partners only when needed by vendors and<br>business partners, with immediate<br>deactivation after use | 12.3.9 Verify that the usage policies require activation of<br>remote-access technologies used by vendors and<br>business partners only when needed by vendors and<br>business partners, with immediate deactivation after use. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
