|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 12.3.10 For personnel accessing cardholder<br>data via remote-access technologies,<br>prohibit the copying, moving, and storage of<br>cardholder data onto local hard drives and<br>removable electronic media, unless explicitly<br>authorized for a defined business need.<br>Where there is an authorized business need,<br>the usage policies must require the data be<br>protected in accordance with all applicable<br>PCI DSS Requirements. | 12.3.10.a Verify that the usage policies prohibit copying,<br>moving, or storing of cardholder data onto local hard<br>drives and removable electronic media when accessing<br>such data via remote-access technologies.<br>For personnel with proper authorization, verify<br>12.3.10.b<br>that usage policies require the protection of cardholder<br>data in accordance with PCI DSS Requirements. | To ensure all personnel are aware of their<br>responsibilities to not store or copy cardholder<br>data onto their local personal computers or other<br>media, your policy should clearly prohibit such<br>activities except for personnel that have been<br>explicitly authorized to do so. Storing or copying<br>cardholder data onto a local hard drive or other<br>media must be in accordance with all applicable<br>PCI DSS requirements. |
| 12.4 Ensure that the security policy and<br>procedures clearly define information security                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Verify that information security policies clearly define<br>12.4.a<br>information security responsibilities for all personnel.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Without clearly defined security roles and<br>responsibilities assigned, there could be                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| responsibilities for all personnel.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Interview a sample of responsible personnel to<br>12.4.b<br>verify they understand the security policies.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | inconsistent interaction with the security group,<br>leading to unsecured implementation of<br>technologies or use of outdated or unsecured<br>technologies.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 12.4.1 Additional requirement for service<br>providers only: Executive management                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 12.4.1.a<br>Examine documentation to verify executive<br>management has assigned overall accountability for                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Note: This requirement applies only when the<br>entity being assessed is a service provider.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| shall establish responsibility for the<br>protection of cardholder data and a PCI DSS<br>compliance program to include:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | maintaining the entity's PCI DSS compliance.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Executive management assignment of PCI DSS<br>compliance responsibilities ensures executive                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| •<br>Overall accountability for maintaining<br>PCI DSS compliance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 12.4.1.b<br>Examine the company's PCI DSS charter to<br>verify it outlines the conditions under which the PCI DSS                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | level visibility into the PCI DSS compliance<br>program and allows for the opportunity to ask<br>appropriate questions to determine the                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| •<br>Defining a charter for a PCI DSS<br>compliance program and<br>communication<br>to executive<br>management                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | compliance program is organized<br>and communicated to<br>executive management.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | effectiveness of the program and influence<br>strategic priorities. Overall responsibility for the<br>PCI DSS compliance program may be assigned to<br>individual roles and/or to business units within the<br>organization.                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Executive management may include C-level<br>positions, board of directors, or equivalent. |-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| <b>12.5</b> Assign to an individual or team the following information security management responsibilities:                                                       | <ul> <li>12.5 Examine information security policies and procedures to verify:</li> <li>The formal assignment of information security to a Chief Security Officer or other security-knowledgeable member of management.</li> <li>The following information security responsibilities are specifically and formally assigned:</li> </ul> | Each person or team with responsibilities for information security management should be clearly aware of their responsibilities and related tasks, through specific policy. Without this accountability, gaps in processes may open access into critical resources or cardholder data.  Entities should also consider transition and/or succession plans for key personnel to avoid |
| <b>12.5.1</b> Establish, document, and distribute security policies and procedures.                                                                               | <b>12.5.1</b> Verify that responsibility for establishing, documenting and distributing security policies and procedures is formally assigned.                                                                                                                                                                                         | potential gaps in security assignments, which could result in responsibilities not being assigned and therefore not performed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| <b>12.5.2</b> Monitor and analyze security alerts and information, and distribute to appropriate personnel.                                                       | <b>12.5.2</b> Verify that responsibility for monitoring and analyzing security alerts and distributing information to appropriate information security and business unit management personnel is formally assigned.                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| <b>12.5.3</b> Establish, document, and distribute security incident response and escalation procedures to ensure timely and effective handling of all situations. | <b>12.5.3</b> Verify that responsibility for establishing, documenting, and distributing security incident response and escalation procedures is formally assigned.                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| <b>12.5.4</b> Administer user accounts, including additions, deletions, and modifications.                                                                        | <b>12.5.4</b> Verify that responsibility for administering (adding, deleting, and modifying) user account and authentication management is formally assigned.                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 12.5.5 Monitor and control all access to data.                                                                                                                    | <b>12.5.5</b> Verify that responsibility for monitoring and controlling all access to data is formally assigned.                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 12.6 Implement a formal security awareness program to make all personnel aware of the cardholder data security policy and                                         | <b>12.6.a</b> Review the security awareness program to verify it provides awareness to all personnel about the cardholder data security policy and procedures.                                                                                                                                                                         | If personnel are not educated about their security responsibilities, security safeguards and processes that have been implemented may                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| procedures.                                                                                                                                                       | <b>12.6.b</b> Examine security awareness program procedures and documentation and perform the following:                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | become ineffective through errors or intentional actions.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 12.6.1<br>Educate personnel upon hire and at<br>least annually.<br>Methods can vary depending on the role<br>Note:                                                                                                                                 | 12.6.1.a<br>Verify that the security awareness program<br>provides multiple methods of communicating awareness<br>and educating personnel<br>(for example, posters, letters,<br>memos, web-based training, meetings, and promotions).                                                          | If the security awareness program does not<br>include periodic refresher sessions, key security<br>processes and procedures may be forgotten or<br>bypassed, resulting in exposed critical resources                                                                          |
| of the personnel and their level of access to<br>the cardholder data.                                                                                                                                                                              | Verify that personnel attend security awareness<br>12.6.1.b<br>training upon hire and at least annually.                                                                                                                                                                                       | and cardholder data.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Interview a sample of personnel to verify they<br>12.6.1.c<br>have completed awareness training and are aware of the<br>importance of cardholder data security.                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Require personnel<br>to acknowledge at<br>12.6.2<br>least annually that they have read and<br>understood the security policy and<br>procedures.                                                                                                    | Verify that the security awareness program<br>12.6.2<br>requires personnel<br>to acknowledge, in writing or<br>electronically, at least annually,<br>that they have read and<br>understand the information security policy.                                                                    | Requiring an acknowledgement by personnel in<br>writing or electronically helps ensure that they<br>have read and understood the security<br>policies/procedures, and that they have made and<br>will continue to make a commitment to comply<br>with these policies.         |
| Screen potential personnel<br>prior to hire to<br>12.7<br>minimize the risk of attacks from internal<br>sources. (Examples of background checks<br>include previous employment history, criminal<br>record, credit history, and reference checks.) | Inquire with Human Resource department<br>12.7<br>management and verify that background checks are<br>conducted (within the constraints of local laws) prior to hire<br>on potential personnel who will have access to cardholder<br>data or the cardholder data environment.                  | Performing thorough background investigations<br>prior to hiring potential personnel who are<br>expected to be given access to cardholder data<br>reduces the risk of unauthorized use of PANs and<br>other cardholder data by individuals with                               |
| Note:<br>For those potential personnel<br>to be hired<br>for certain positions such as store cashiers<br>who only have access to one card number at a<br>time when facilitating a transaction, this<br>requirement is a recommendation only.       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | questionable or criminal backgrounds.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Maintain and implement policies and<br>12.8<br>procedures to manage service providers with<br>whom<br>cardholder data is shared, or that could<br>affect the security of cardholder data, as<br>follows:                                           | Through observation, review of policies and<br>12.8<br>procedures, and review of supporting documentation, verify<br>that processes are implemented to manage service<br>providers with whom<br>cardholder data is shared, or that<br>could affect the security of cardholder data as follows: | If a merchant or service provider shares<br>cardholder data with a service provider, certain<br>requirements apply to ensure continued protection<br>of this data will be enforced by such service<br>providers.                                                              |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Some examples of the different types of service<br>providers include<br>backup<br>tape storage facilities,<br>managed service providers<br>such as web-hosting<br>companies<br>or security service providers, entities<br>that receive data for fraud-modeling purposes, etc. |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Maintain a list of service providers<br>12.8.1<br>including a description of the service<br>provided.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Verify that a list of service providers is maintained<br>12.8.1<br>and includes a description of the service provided.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Keeping track of all service providers identifies<br>where potential risk extends to outside of the<br>organization.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| 12.8.2 Maintain a written agreement that<br>includes an acknowledgement that the<br>service providers are responsible for the<br>security of cardholder data the service<br>providers possess or otherwise store,<br>process or transmit on behalf of the<br>customer, or to the extent that they could<br>impact the security of the customer's<br>cardholder data environment. | Observe written agreements and confirm they<br>12.8.2<br>include an acknowledgement by service providers that<br>they are responsible for the security of cardholder data<br>the service providers possess or otherwise store, process<br>or transmit on behalf of the customer, or to the extent that<br>they could impact the security of the customer's<br>cardholder data environment. | The acknowledgement of the service providers<br>evidences their commitment to maintaining proper<br>security of cardholder data that it obtains from its<br>clients.<br>The extent to which the service provider<br>is<br>responsible for the security of cardholder data will<br>depend on the particular service and the<br>agreement between the provider and assessed<br>entity.<br>In conjunction with Requirement 12.9, this |
| Note: The exact wording of an<br>acknowledgement will depend on the<br>agreement between the two parties, the details<br>of the service being provided, and the<br>responsibilities assigned to each party. The<br>acknowledgement does not have to include the<br>exact wording provided in this requirement.                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | requirement is intended to promote a consistent<br>level of understanding between parties about their<br>applicable PCI DSS responsibilities. For example,<br>the agreement may include the<br>applicable PCI<br>DSS requirements<br>to be maintained as part of the<br>provided service.                                                                                                                                          |
| 12.8.3 Ensure there is an established<br>process for engaging service providers<br>including proper due diligence prior to<br>engagement.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | 12.8.3<br>Verify that policies and procedures are<br>documented and implemented including proper due<br>diligence prior to engaging any service provider.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | The process ensures that any engagement of a<br>service provider is thoroughly vetted internally by<br>an organization, which should include a risk<br>analysis prior to establishing a formal relationship<br>with the service provider.                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Specific due-diligence processes and goals will vary<br>for each organization. Examples of considerations<br>may include the provider's reporting practices,<br>breach-notification and incident response<br>procedures, details of how PCI DSS responsibilities<br>are assigned between each party, how the provider<br>validates their PCI DSS compliance and what<br>evidence they<br>will<br>provide, etc.                     |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

| Maintain information about which PCI<br>12.8.5<br>DSS requirements are managed by each<br>service provider, and which are managed by<br>the entity.                                                                                                                                                               | Verify the entity maintains information about which<br>12.8.5<br>PCI DSS requirements are managed by each service<br>provider, and which are managed by the entity.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |

| are responsible for the security of cardholder<br>data the service provider possesses or<br>otherwise stores, processes, or transmits on<br>behalf of the<br>customer, or to the extent that<br>they could impact the security of the<br>customer's cardholder data environment.<br>Note: The exact wording of an |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | In conjunction with Requirement 12.8.2, this<br>requirement is intended to promote a consistent<br>level of understanding between service providers<br>and their customers about their applicable PCI<br>DSS responsibilities. The acknowledgement of the<br>service providers evidences their commitment to<br>maintaining proper security of cardholder data that                                              |
| acknowledgement will depend on the<br>agreement between the two parties, the details<br>of the service being provided, and the<br>responsibilities assigned to each party. The method by which the service<br>provider provides written acknowledgment should                                       |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | be agreed between the provider and their<br>customers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

| PCI DSS Requirements                                                                                                | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                          | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

### <span id="page-115-0"></span>**Appendix A: Additional PCI DSS Requirements**

This appendix contains additional PCI DSS requirements for different types of entities. The sections within this Appendix include:

- Appendix A1: Additional PCI DSS Requirements for Shared Hosting Providers
- Appendix A2: Additional PCI DSS Requirements for Entities using SSL/early TLS for Card-Present POS POI terminal connections
- Appendix A3: Designated Entities Supplemental Validation

Guidance and applicability information is provided within each section.

{116}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_116_Picture_0.jpeg)

#### <span id="page-116-0"></span>*Appendix A1: Additional PCI DSS Requirements for Shared Hosting Providers*

As referenced in Requirement 12.8 and 12.9, all service providers with access to cardholder data (including shared hosting providers) must adhere to the PCI DSS. In addition, Requirement 2.6 states that shared hosting providers must protect each entity's hosted environment and data. Therefore, shared hosting providers must additionally comply with the requirements in this Appendix.

| A1 Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A1 Protect each<br>entity's (that is,<br>merchant, service provider, or other<br>entity) hosted environment and data, per<br>A1.1 through A1.4:                                                                                                    | A1<br>Specifically for a PCI DSS assessment of a shared hosting<br>provider, to verify that shared hosting providers protect entities'<br>(merchants and service providers) hosted environment and data,<br>select a sample of servers (Microsoft Windows and Unix/Linux)<br>across a representative sample of hosted merchants and service<br>providers,<br>and perform A1.1 through A1.4 below: | Appendix A<br>of PCI DSS is intended for<br>shared hosting providers who wish to<br>provide their merchant and/or service<br>provider customers with a PCI DSS                                                        |
| A hosting provider must fulfill these<br>requirements as well as all other relevant<br>sections of the PCI DSS.                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | compliant hosting environment.                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| Even though a hosting provider<br>Note:<br>may meet these requirements, the<br>compliance of the entity that uses the<br>hosting provider is not guaranteed. Each<br>entity must comply with the PCI DSS and<br>validate compliance as applicable. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| Ensure that each entity only runs<br>A1.1<br>processes that have access to that<br>entity's cardholder data environment.                                                                                                                           | A1.1 If a shared hosting provider allows entities (for example,<br>merchants or service providers) to run their own applications, verify<br>these application processes run using the unique ID of the entity.<br>For example:<br>•<br>No entity on the system can use a shared web server user ID.<br>•<br>All CGI scripts used by an entity must be created and run as                          | If a merchant or service provider is<br>allowed to run their own applications on<br>the shared server, these should run with<br>the user ID of the merchant or service<br>provider, rather than as a privileged user. |

{117}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_117_Picture_0.jpeg)

| A1 Requirements                                                                                                                                                  | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Restrict<br>each entity's access and<br>A1.2<br>privileges to its own cardholder data<br>environment only.                                                       | A1.2.a Verify the user ID of any application process is not a<br>privileged user (root/admin).<br>A1.2.b Verify each entity (merchant, service provider) has read,<br>write, or execute permissions only for files and directories it owns or<br>for necessary system files (restricted via file system permissions,<br>access control lists, chroot, jailshell, etc.)<br>Important:<br>An entity's files may not be shared by group.<br>A1.2.c Verify that an entity's users do not have write access to<br>shared system binaries.<br>A1.2.d Verify that<br>viewing of log entries is restricted to the owning<br>entity.<br>A1.2.e To ensure each entity cannot monopolize server resources<br>to exploit vulnerabilities (for example, error, race, and restart<br>conditions resulting in, for example, buffer overflows), verify<br>restrictions are in place for the use of these system resources:<br>•<br>Disk space<br>•<br>Bandwidth<br>•<br>Memory<br>•<br>CPU | To ensure that access and privileges are<br>restricted such that each merchant or<br>service provider has access only to their<br>own environment, consider the following:<br>1.<br>Privileges of the merchant's or service<br>provider's web server user ID;<br>2.<br>Permissions granted to read, write,<br>and execute files;<br>3.<br>Permissions granted to write to system<br>binaries;<br>4.<br>Permissions granted to merchant's<br>and service provider's log files; and<br>5.<br>Controls to ensure one merchant or<br>service provider cannot monopolize<br>system resources. |
| A1.3 Ensure logging and audit trails<br>are enabled and unique to each entity's<br>cardholder data environment and<br>consistent with PCI DSS Requirement<br>10. | A1.3 Verify the shared hosting provider has enabled logging as<br>follows, for each merchant and service provider environment:<br>•<br>Logs are enabled for common third-party applications.<br>•<br>Logs are active by default.<br>•<br>Logs are available for review by the owning entity.<br>•<br>Log locations are clearly communicated to the owning entity.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Logs should be available in a shared<br>hosting environment so the merchants and<br>service providers have access to, and can<br>review, logs specific to their cardholder<br>data environment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| A1.4<br>Enable processes to provide for<br>timely forensic investigation in the<br>event of a compromise to any hosted<br>merchant or service provider.          | A1.4 Verify the shared hosting provider has written policies that<br>provide for a timely forensics investigation of related servers in the<br>event of a compromise.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Shared hosting providers must have<br>processes to provide quick and easy<br>response in the event that a forensic<br>investigation is needed for a compromise,<br>down to the appropriate level of detail so<br>that an individual merchant's or service<br>provider's details are available.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

#### <span id="page-118-0"></span>*Appendix A2: Additional PCI DSS Requirements for Entities using SSL/Early TLS for Card-Present POS POI Terminal Connections*

Entities using SSL and early TLS for POS POI terminal connections must work toward upgrading to a strong cryptographic protocol as soon as possible. Additionally, SSL and/or early TLS must not be introduced into environments where those protocols don't already exist. At the time of publication, the known vulnerabilities are difficult to exploit in POS POI payment terminals. However, new vulnerabilities could emerge at any time, and it is up to the organization to remain up to date with vulnerability trends and determine whether or not they are susceptible to any known exploits.

The PCI DSS requirements directly affected are:

**Requirement 2.2.3** Implement additional security features for any required services, protocols, or daemons that are

considered to be insecure.

**Requirement 2.3** Encrypt all non-console administrative access using strong cryptography.

**Requirement 4.1** Use strong cryptography and security protocols to safeguard sensitive cardholder data during

transmission over open, public networks.

SSL and early TLS must not be used as a security control to meet these requirements, except in the case of POS POI terminal connections as detailed in this appendix. To support entities working to migrate away from SSL/early TLS on POS POI terminals, the following provisions are included:

- New POS POI terminal implementations must not use SSL or early TLS as a security control.
- All POS POI terminal service providers must provide a secure service offering.
- Service providers supporting existing POS POI terminal implementations that use SSL and/or early TLS must have a formal Risk Mitigation and Migration Plan in place.
- POS POI terminals in card-present environments that can be verified as not being susceptible to any known exploits for SSL and early TLS, **and the SSL/TLS termination points to which they connect,** may continue using SSL/early TLS as a security control.

This Appendix only applies to entities using SSL/early TLS as a security control to protect POS POI terminals, including service providers who provide connections into POS POI terminals.

|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A2.2<br>Requirement for Service<br>All service providers<br>Providers Only:<br>with existing<br>connection points to POS<br>POI terminals<br>referred to in A2.1 that use<br>SSL and/or early TLS must have a<br>formal Risk Mitigation and Migration Plan<br>in place. | A2.2 Review the documented Risk Mitigation and Migration Plan<br>to verify it includes:<br>•<br>Description of usage, including what data is being<br>transmitted, types and number of systems that use<br>and/or support SSL/early TLS, type of environment;<br>•<br>Risk-assessment results and risk-reduction controls in<br>place;<br>•<br>Description of processes to monitor for new<br>vulnerabilities associated with SSL/early TLS;<br>•<br>Description of change control processes that are<br>implemented to ensure SSL/early TLS is not<br>implemented into new environments;<br>•<br>Overview of migration project plan to replace SSL/early<br>TLS at a future date. | POS POI termination points, including but not<br>limited to a service providers such as an<br>acquirer or acquirer processor, can continue<br>using SSL/early TLS when it can be shown that<br>the service provider has controls in place that<br>mitigate the risk of supporting those connections<br>for the service provider environment.<br>The Risk Mitigation and Migration Plan is a<br>document prepared by the entity that details<br>their plans for migrating to a secure protocol,<br>and also describes controls the entity has in<br>place to reduce the risk associated with<br>SSL/early TLS until the migration is complete.<br>Service providers<br>should communicate to all<br>customers using SSL/early TLS about the risks<br>associated with its use and need to migrate to a<br>secure protocol.<br>Refer to the current PCI SSC Information<br>Supplements on SSL/early TLS<br>for further<br>guidance on Risk Mitigation and Migration Plans. |

#### <span id="page-121-0"></span>*Appendix A3: Designated Entities Supplemental Validation (DESV)*

This Appendix applies only to entities designated by a payment brand(s) or acquirer as requiring additional validation of existing PCI DSS requirements. Examples of entities that this Appendix *could* apply to include:

- Those storing, processing, and/or transmitting large volumes of cardholder data,
- Those providing aggregation points for cardholder data, or
- Those that have suffered significant or repeated breaches of cardholder data.

These supplemental validation steps are intended to provide greater assurance that PCI DSS controls are maintained effectively and on a continuous basis through validation of business-as-usual (BAU) processes, and increased validation and scoping consideration.

The additional validation steps in this document are organized into the following control areas:

- *A3.1 Implement a PCI DSS compliance program.*
- *A3.2 Document and validate PCI DSS scope.*
- *A3.3 Validate PCI DSS is incorporated into business-as-usual (BAU) activities.*
- *A3.4 Control and manage logical access to the cardholder data environment.*
- *A3.5 Identify and respond to suspicious events.*

*Note: Some requirements have defined timeframes (for example, at least quarterly or every six months) within which certain activities are to be performed. For initial assessment to this document, it is not required that an activity has been performed for every such timeframe during the previous year, if the assessor verifies:*

- *1) The activity was performed in accordance with the applicable requirement within the most recent timeframe (that is, the most recent quarter or six-month period), and*
- *2) The entity has documented policies and procedures for continuing to perform the activity within the defined timeframe. For subsequent years after the initial assessment, an activity must have been performed for each timeframe for which it is required (for example, a quarterly activity must have been performed for each of the previous year's four quarters).*

*Note: An entity is required to undergo an assessment according to this Appendix ONLY if instructed to do so by an acquirer or a payment brand.*

{122}------------------------------------------------

|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A3.1 Implement a PCI DSS compliance program                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
