Overall responsibility for the<br>PCI DSS compliance program may be assigned to<br>individual roles and/or to business units within the<br>organization.                                                                                                                                                          |
| •<br>Overall accountability for maintaining PCI<br>DSS compliance<br>•<br>Defining a charter for a PCI DSS<br>compliance program                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | A3.1.1.b Examine the company's<br>PCI DSS charter to<br>verify it outlines the conditions under which<br>the PCI DSS<br>compliance program is organized.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| •<br>Providing updates to executive<br>management and board of directors on<br>PCI DSS compliance initiatives and<br>issues, including remediation activities, at<br>least annually                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | A3.1.1.c Examine executive management and board of<br>directors meeting minutes and/or presentations to<br>ensure PCI DSS compliance initiatives and remediation<br>activities are communicated at least annually.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Requirement 12<br>PCI DSS Reference:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| A3.1.2<br>A formal PCI DSS compliance program<br>must be in place to include:<br>•<br>Definition of activities for maintaining and<br>monitoring overall PCI DSS compliance,<br>including business-as-usual activities<br>•<br>Annual PCI DSS assessment processes<br>•<br>Processes for the continuous validation of<br>PCI DSS requirements (for example:<br>daily, weekly, quarterly, etc. as applicable<br>per requirement)<br>•<br>A process for performing business-impact<br>analysis to determine potential PCI DSS<br>impacts for strategic business decisions | A3.1.2.a<br>Examine information security policies and<br>procedures to verify that processes are specifically<br>defined for the following:<br>•<br>Maintaining and monitoring overall PCI DSS<br>compliance, including business-as-usual activities<br>•<br>Annual PCI DSS assessment(s)<br>•<br>Continuous validation of PCI DSS requirements<br>•<br>Business-impact analysis to determine potential PCI<br>DSS impacts for strategic business decisions | A formal compliance program allows an<br>organization to monitor the health of its security<br>controls, be proactive in the event that a control<br>fails, and effectively communicate activities and<br>compliance status throughout the organization.<br>The PCI DSS compliance program can be a<br>dedicated program or part of an over-arching<br>compliance and/or governance program, and<br>should include a well-defined methodology that<br>demonstrates consistent and effective evaluation.<br>Example methodologies include: Deming Circle of<br>Plan-Do-Check-Act (PDCA), ISO 27001, COBIT,<br>DMAIC, and Six Sigma. |
| PCI DSS Reference:<br>Requirements 1-12                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | (Continued on next page)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

{123}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_123_Picture_0.jpeg)

| Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A3.1.2.b<br>Interview personnel and observe compliance<br>activities to verify that the defined processes are<br>implemented for the following:<br>•<br>Maintaining and monitoring overall PCI DSS<br>compliance, including business-as-usual activities<br>•<br>Annual PCI DSS assessment(s)<br>•<br>Continuous validation of PCI DSS requirements<br>•<br>Business-impact analysis to determine potential PCI<br>DSS impacts for strategic business decisions                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Maintaining and monitoring an organization's<br>overall PCI DSS compliance includes identifying<br>activities to be performed daily, weekly, monthly,<br>quarterly, or annually, and ensuring these<br>activities are being performed accordingly (for<br>example, using a security self-assessment or<br>PDCA methodology).<br>Examples of strategic business decisions that<br>should be analyzed for potential PCI DSS impacts<br>may include mergers and acquisitions, new<br>technology purchases, or new payment<br>acceptance channels.                                                    |
| A3.1.3.a<br>Examine information security policies and<br>procedures and interview personnel to verify that roles<br>and responsibilities are clearly defined and that duties<br>are assigned to include at least the following:<br>•<br>Managing PCI DSS business-as-usual activities<br>•<br>Managing annual PCI DSS assessments<br>•<br>Managing continuous validation of PCI DSS<br>requirements (for example: daily, weekly, quarterly,<br>etc. as applicable per requirement)<br>•<br>Managing business-impact analysis to determine<br>potential PCI DSS impacts for strategic business<br>decisions<br>Interview responsible personnel and verify they<br>A3.1.3.b | The formal definition of specific PCI DSS<br>compliance roles and responsibilities helps to<br>ensure accountability and monitoring of ongoing<br>PCI DSS compliance efforts. These roles may be<br>assigned to a single owner or multiple owners for<br>different aspects. Ownership should be assigned<br>to individuals with the authority to make risk<br>based decisions and upon whom accountability<br>rests for the specific function. Duties should be<br>formally defined and owners should be able to<br>demonstrate an understanding of their<br>responsibilities and accountability. |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | are familiar with and performing their designated PCI<br>DSS compliance responsibilities.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |

| A3 Requirements                                                                                                                                                                       | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Provide up-to-date PCI DSS and/or<br>A3.1.4<br>information security training at least annually<br>to personnel with PCI DSS compliance<br>responsibilities (as identified in A3.1.3). | Examine information security policies and<br>A3.1.4.a<br>procedures to verify that PCI DSS and/or information<br>security training is required at least annually for each<br>role with PCI DSS compliance responsibilities.                                    | Personnel responsible for PCI DSS compliance<br>have specific training needs exceeding that which<br>is typically provided by general security<br>awareness training. Individuals with PCI DSS                                                                                                                                                |
| Requirement 12<br>PCI DSS Reference:                                                                                                                                                  | A3.1.4.b Interview personnel and examine certificates of<br>attendance or<br>other records to verify that personnel with<br>PCI DSS compliance responsibility receive up-to-date<br>PCI DSS and/or similar information security training at<br>least annually. | compliance responsibilities should receive<br>specialized training that, in addition to general<br>awareness of information security, focuses on<br>specific security topics, skills, processes, or<br>methodologies that must be followed for those<br>individuals to perform their compliance<br>responsibilities effectively.              |
|                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Training may be offered by third parties—for<br>example, SANS or PCI SSC (PCI Awareness,<br>PCIP, and ISA), payment brands,<br>and acquirers—<br>or training may be internal. Training content<br>should be applicable for the particular job function<br>and be current to include the latest security<br>threats and/or version of PCI DSS. |
|                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | For additional guidance on<br>developing appropriate<br>security training content for specialized roles, refer<br>to the PCI SSC's Information Supplement on Best<br>Practices for Implementing a Security Awareness<br>Program.                                                                                                              |

| A3 Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A3.2 Document and validate PCI DSS scope                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Document and confirm the accuracy of<br>A3.2.1<br>PCI DSS scope at least quarterly and upon<br>significant changes to the in-scope<br>environment. At a minimum, the quarterly<br>scoping validation should include:<br>•<br>Identifying all in-scope networks and<br>system components<br>•<br>Identifying all out-of-scope networks and                                                                                                                                                       | A3.2.1.a Examine documented results of scope reviews<br>and interview personnel to verify that the reviews are<br>performed:<br>•<br>At least quarterly<br>•<br>After significant changes to the in-scope<br>environment<br>Examine documented results of quarterly scope<br>A3.2.1.b                                                                                                                                                                         | Validation of PCI DSS scope should be performed<br>as frequently as possible to ensure PCI DSS<br>scope remains up to date and aligned with<br>changing business objectives.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| justification for networks being out of<br>scope, including descriptions of all<br>segmentation controls implemented<br>•<br>Identifying all connected entities—e.g.,<br>third-party entities with access to the<br>cardholder data environment (CDE)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | reviews<br>to verify the following is performed:<br>•<br>Identification of all in-scope networks and system<br>components<br>•<br>Identification of all out-of-scope networks and<br>justification for networks being out of scope,<br>including descriptions of all segmentation controls<br>implemented                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Scope of PCI DSS<br>PCI DSS Reference:<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | •<br>Identification of all connected entities—e.g., third<br>party entities with access to the CDE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| A3.2.2 Determine PCI DSS scope impact for<br>all changes to systems or networks, including<br>additions of new systems and new network<br>connections. Processes must include:<br>•<br>Performing a formal PCI DSS impact<br>assessment<br>•<br>Identifying applicable PCI DSS<br>requirements to the system or network<br>•<br>Updating PCI DSS scope as appropriate<br>•<br>Documented sign-off of the results of the<br>impact assessment by responsible<br>personnel (as defined in A3.1.3) | A3.2.2 Examine<br>change documentation and interview<br>personnel to verify that for each change to systems or<br>networks:<br>•<br>A formal PCI DSS impact assessment was<br>performed.<br>•<br>PCI DSS requirements applicable to the<br>system or<br>network changes were identified.<br>•<br>PCI DSS scope was updated as appropriate for the<br>change.<br>•<br>Sign-off by responsible personnel (as defined in<br>A3.1.3) was obtained and documented. | Changes to systems or networks can have<br>significant impact to PCI DSS scope. For<br>example, firewall rule changes can bring whole<br>network segments into scope, or new systems<br>may be added to the CDE that have to be<br>appropriately protected.<br>Processes to determine the potential impact that<br>changes to systems and networks may have on<br>an entity's PCI DSS scope may be performed as<br>part of a dedicated PCI DSS compliance program,<br>or may fall under an entity's over-arching<br>compliance and/or governance program. |
| PCI DSS Reference: Scope of PCI DSS<br>Requirements; Requirements 1-12                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A3.2.2.1<br>changes, examine change records, interview personnel<br>and observe the affected systems/networks to verify<br>that applicable PCI DSS requirements were<br>implemented and documentation updated as part of | It is important to have processes to analyze all<br>changes made to ensure that all appropriate PCI<br>DSS controls are applied to any systems or<br>networks added to the in-scope environment due<br>to a change. |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Building this validation into change management<br>processes helps ensure that device inventories                                                                                                                   |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | and configuration standards are kept up to date<br>and security controls are applied where needed.                                                                                                                  |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | A change<br>management process should include<br>supporting evidence that PCI DSS requirements<br>are implemented or preserved through the<br>iterative process.                                                    |

| A3 Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A3.2.3 Changes to organizational structure—<br>for example, a company merger or acquisition,<br>change or reassignment of<br>personnel with<br>responsibility for security controls—result in a<br>formal (internal) review of the impact to PCI<br>DSS scope and applicability of controls.<br>PCI DSS Reference: Requirement 12 | A3.2.3 Examine policies and procedures to verify that a<br>change to organizational structure results in formal<br>review of the impact to PCI DSS scope and applicability<br>of controls.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | An organization's structure and management<br>define the requirements and protocol for effective<br>and secure operations. Changes to this structure<br>could<br>have negative effects to existing controls<br>and frameworks by reallocating or removing<br>resources that once supported PCI DSS controls<br>or inheriting new responsibilities that may not<br>have established controls in place. Therefore, it is<br>important to revisit PCI DSS scope and controls<br>when there are changes to ensure controls are in<br>place and active. |
| A3.2.4 If segmentation is used, confirm PCI<br>DSS scope by performing penetration testing<br>on segmentation controls at least every six<br>months and after any changes to<br>segmentation controls/methods.<br>Requirement 11<br>PCI DSS Reference:                                                                            | A3.2.4 Examine the results from the most recent<br>penetration test to verify that:<br>•<br>Penetration testing is performed to verify<br>segmentation controls<br>at least every six months and<br>after any changes to segmentation<br>controls/methods.<br>•<br>The penetration testing covers all segmentation<br>controls/methods in use.<br>•<br>The penetration testing verifies that segmentation<br>controls/methods are operational and effective, and<br>isolate all out-of-scope systems from systems in the<br>CDE. | If segmentation is used to isolate in-scope<br>networks from out-of-scope networks, those<br>segmentation controls must be verified using<br>penetration testing to confirm they continue to<br>operate as intended and effectively. Penetration<br>testing<br>techniques should follow the existing<br>penetration methodology as specified in PCI DSS<br>Requirement 11.<br>For additional information on effective penetration<br>testing, refer to the PCI SSC's Information<br>Supplement on Penetration Testing Guidance.                    |

{128}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_128_Picture_0.jpeg)

| A3 Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A3.2.5 Implement a data-discovery<br>methodology to confirm PCI DSS scope and to<br>locate all sources and locations of clear-text<br>PAN at least quarterly and upon significant<br>changes to the cardholder environment or<br>processes.<br>Data-discovery methodology must take into<br>consideration the potential for clear-text PAN<br>to reside on systems and networks outside of<br>the currently defined CDE.<br>PCI DSS Reference:<br>Scope of PCI DSS<br>Requirements | A3.2.5.a Examine documented data-discovery<br>methodology to verify the following:<br>•<br>Data-discovery methodology includes processes for<br>identifying all sources and locations of clear-text<br>PAN.<br>•<br>Methodology takes into consideration the potential<br>for clear-text PAN to reside on systems and<br>networks outside of the currently defined CDE.<br>A3.2.5.b Examine results from recent data discovery<br>efforts, and interview responsible personnel to verify that<br>data discovery is performed at least quarterly and upon<br>significant changes to the cardholder environment or<br>processes. | PCI DSS<br>requires that, as part of the scoping<br>exercise, assessed entities must identify and<br>document the existence of all clear-text PAN in<br>their environments. Implementing a data<br>discovery methodology that identifies all sources<br>and locations of clear-text PAN, and takes into<br>consideration the potential for clear-text PAN to<br>reside on systems and networks outside of the<br>currently defined CDE or in unexpected places<br>within the defined CDE—for example, in an error<br>log or memory dump file—<br>helps to ensure that<br>previously unknown locations of clear-text PAN<br>are detected and properly secured.<br>A data-discovery process can be performed via a<br>variety of methods, including but not limited to: (1)<br>commercially available data-discovery software,<br>(2) an in-house developed data-discovery<br>program, or (3) a manual search. Regardless of<br>the method used, the goal of the effort is to find all<br>sources and locations of clear-text PAN (not just<br>in the defined CDE). |
| A3.2.5.1 Ensure effectiveness of methods<br>used for data discovery—–e.g., methods<br>must be<br>able to discover clear-text PAN on<br>all types of system components (for<br>example, on each operating system or<br>platform) and file formats in use.<br>The effectiveness of data-discovery methods<br>must be confirmed at least annually.<br>PCI DSS Reference:<br>Scope of PCI<br>DSS<br>Requirements                                                                       | A3.2.5.1.a Interview personnel and review<br>documentation to verify:<br><br>The entity has a process in place to test the<br>effectiveness of methods used for data discovery.<br><br>The process includes verifying the methods are<br>able to discover clear-text PAN on all types of<br>system components and file formats in use.<br>A3.2.5.1.b Examine the results of recent effectiveness<br>tests to verify the effectiveness of methods used for<br>data discovery is confirmed at least annually.                                                                                                                  | A process to test the effectiveness of the methods<br>used for data discovery ensures the completeness<br>and accuracy of cardholder data detection. For<br>completeness, at least a sampling of system<br>components in both the in-scope and out-of-scope<br>networks should be included in the data-discovery<br>process. Accuracy can be tested by placing test<br>PANs on a sample of system components and file<br>formats in use and confirming that the data<br>discovery<br>method detected the test PANs.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

| A3 Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |  |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--|
| A3.2.5.2 Implement response procedures to<br>be initiated upon the detection of clear-text<br>PAN outside of the CDE to include:<br><br>Procedures for determining what to do<br>if clear-text PAN is discovered outside<br>of the CDE, including its retrieval,<br>secure deletion and/or migration into<br>the currently defined CDE, as<br>applicable<br><br>Procedures for determining how the<br>data ended up outside of the CDE<br><br>Procedures for remediating data leaks<br>or process gaps that resulted in the<br>data being outside of the CDE<br><br>Procedures for identifying the source of<br>the data<br><br>Procedures for identifying whether<br>any<br>track data is stored with the PANs | Examine documented response procedures<br>A3.2.5.2.a<br>to verify that procedures for responding to the<br>detection of clear-text PAN outside of the CDE are<br>defined and include:<br><br>Procedures for determining what to do if clear<br>text PAN is discovered outside of the CDE,<br>including its retrieval, secure deletion and/or<br>migration into the currently defined CDE, as<br>applicable<br><br>Procedures for determining how the data ended<br>up outside the CDE<br><br>Procedures for remediating data leaks or process<br>gaps that resulted in the data being outside of the<br>CDE<br><br>Procedures for identifying the source of the data<br><br>Procedures for identifying whether any track data<br>is stored with the PANs<br>A3.2.5.2.b Interview personnel and examine records of<br>response actions to verify that remediation activities<br>are performed when clear-text PAN is detected outside | Having documented response procedures that are<br>followed in the event clear-text PAN is found<br>outside of the CDE helps to identify the necessary<br>remediation actions and prevent future leaks. For<br>example, if PAN was found outside of the CDE,<br>analysis should be performed to (1) determine<br>whether it was saved independently of<br>other data<br>(or was it part of a full track?), (2) to identify the<br>source of the data, and (3) identify the control<br>gaps that resulted in the data being outside the<br>CDE. |  |
| Implement mechanisms for detecting<br>A3.2.6<br>and preventing clear-text PAN from leaving the<br>CDE via an unauthorized channel, method, or<br>process, including generation of audit logs and<br>alerts.<br>Scope of PCI DSS<br>PCI DSS Reference:<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | of the CDE.<br>A3.2.6.a Examine documentation and observe<br>implemented mechanisms to verify that the mechanisms<br>are:<br>•<br>Implemented and actively running<br>•<br>Configured to detect and prevent clear-text<br>PAN<br>leaving the CDE via an unauthorized channel,<br>method, or process<br>•<br>Generating logs and alerts upon detection of clear<br>text PAN leaving the CDE via an unauthorized<br>channel, method, or process<br>A3.2.6.b Examine audit logs and alerts, and interview<br>responsible personnel to verify that alerts are<br>investigated.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Mechanisms to detect and prevent unauthorized<br>loss of clear-text PAN may include appropriate<br>tools—such as data loss prevention (DLP)<br>solutions—and/or manual processes and<br>procedures. Coverage of the mechanisms should<br>include, but not be limited to, e-mails, downloads<br>to removable media, and output to printers. Use of<br>these mechanisms allows an organization to<br>detect and prevent situations that may lead to<br>data loss.                                                                               |  |

![](_page_130_Picture_0.jpeg)

| A3 Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A3.2.6.1 Implement response procedures to<br>be initiated upon the detection of attempts to<br>remove clear-text PAN from the CDE via an<br>unauthorized channel, method, or process.<br>Response procedures must include:<br><br>Procedures for the timely investigation<br>of alerts by responsible personnel<br><br>Procedures for remediating data leaks | Examine documented response procedures<br>A3.2.6.1.a<br>to verify that procedures for responding to the<br>attempted removal of clear-text PAN from the CDE via<br>an unauthorized channel, method, or process include:<br><br>Procedures for the timely investigation of alerts<br>by responsible personnel<br><br>Procedures for remediating data leaks or process<br>gaps, as necessary, to prevent any data loss | Attempts to remove clear-text PAN via an<br>unauthorized channel, method, or process may<br>indicate malicious intent to steal data, or may be<br>the actions of an authorized employee who is<br>unaware of or simply not following the proper<br>methods. Timely investigation of these<br>occurrences can identify where remediation needs<br>to be applied and provides valuable information to<br>help understand where the threats are coming |
| or process gaps, as necessary, to<br>prevent any data loss                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | A3.2.6.1.b<br>Interview personnel and examine records of<br>actions taken when clear-text PAN is detected leaving<br>the CDE via an unauthorized channel, method, or<br>process, and verify that remediation activities were<br>performed.                                                                                                                                                                             | from.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| A3.3 Validate PCI DSS is incorporated into business-as-usual (BAU) activities                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| A3.3.1 Implement a process to immediately<br>detect and alert on critical security control<br>failures. Examples of critical security controls                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Examine documented policies and procedures<br>A3.3.1.a<br>to verify that processes are defined to immediately<br>detect and alert on critical security control failures.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Without formal processes for the prompt (as soon<br>as possible) detection and alerting of critical<br>security control failures, failures may go<br>undetected for extended periods and provide<br>attackers ample time to compromise systems and<br>steal sensitive data from the cardholder data<br>environment.                                                                                                                                 |
| include, but are not limited to:<br>•<br>Firewalls<br>•<br>IDS/IPS<br>•<br>FIM<br>•<br>Anti-virus<br>•<br>Physical access controls<br>•<br>Logical access controls<br>•<br>Audit logging mechanisms                                                                                                                                                            | A3.3.1.b Examine detection and alerting processes and<br>interview personnel to verify that processes are<br>implemented for all critical security controls, and that<br>failure of a critical security control results in the<br>generation of an alert.                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| •<br>Segmentation controls<br>(if used)<br>PCI DSS Reference: Requirements 1-12                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |

| Testing Procedures<br>A3 Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| A3.3.1.1 Respond to failures of any critical<br>security controls in a timely manner.<br>Processes for responding to failures in<br>security controls must include:<br><br>Restoring security functions<br><br>Identifying and documenting the<br>duration (date and time start to end) of<br>the security failure<br><br>Identifying and documenting cause(s)<br>of failure, including root cause, and<br>documenting remediation required to<br>address root cause<br><br>Identifying and addressing any security<br>issues that arose during the failure | A3.3.1.1.a<br>Examine documented policies and<br>procedures and interview personnel to verify processes<br>are defined and implemented to respond to a security<br>control failure, and include:<br><br>Restoring security functions<br><br>Identifying and documenting the duration (date<br>and time start to end) of the security failure<br><br>Identifying and documenting cause(s) of failure,<br>including root cause, and documenting<br>remediation required to address root cause<br><br>Identifying and addressing any security issues<br>that arose during the failure<br><br>Performing a risk assessment to determine<br>whether further actions are required as a result of | Documented evidence (e.g.,<br>records within a<br>problem-management system) should support<br>that processes and procedures are in place to<br>respond to security failures. In addition, personnel<br>should be aware of their responsibilities in the<br>event of a failure. Actions and responses to the<br>failure should be captured in the documented<br>evidence. |
| <br>Performing a risk assessment to<br>determine whether further actions are<br>required as a result of the security<br>failure                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | the security failure<br><br>Implementing controls to prevent cause of failure<br>from reoccurring                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| <br>Implementing controls to prevent cause                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | <br>Resuming monitoring of security controls                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| of failure from reoccurring<br><br>Resuming monitoring of security<br>controls                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Examine records to verify that security<br>A3.3.1.1.b<br>control failures are documented to include:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

| Review hardware and software<br>A3.3.2<br>technologies at least annually to confirm<br>whether they continue to meet the<br>organization's PCI DSS requirements. (For<br>example, a review of technologies that are no<br>longer supported by the vendor and/or no<br>longer meet the security needs of the<br>organization.) | A3.3.2.a Examine documented policies and procedures<br>and interview personnel to verify processes are defined<br>and implemented to review hardware and software<br>technologies to confirm whether they continue to meet<br>the organization's PCI DSS requirements. | Hardware and software technologies are<br>constantly evolving, and organizations need to be<br>aware of changes to the technologies they use, as<br>well as the evolving threats to those technologies.<br>Organizations also need to be aware of changes<br>made by technology<br>vendors to their products or<br>support processes, to understand how such<br>changes may impact the organization's use of the<br>technology.<br>Regular reviews of technologies that impact or<br>influence PCI DSS controls can assist with<br>purchasing, usage, and deployment<br>strategies,<br>and ensure controls that rely on those<br>technologies remain effective. |

| Perform reviews at least quarterly<br>A3.3.3<br>to verify BAU activities are being followed.<br>Reviews must be performed by personnel<br>assigned to the PCI DSS compliance<br>program (as identified in A3.1.3), and<br>include the following:<br>•<br>Confirmation that all BAU activities (e.g.,<br>A3.2.2, A3.2.6, and A3.3.1) are being<br>performed<br>•<br>Confirmation that personnel are following<br>security policies and operational<br>procedures<br>(for example, daily log<br>reviews, firewall rule-set reviews,<br>configuration standards for new systems,<br>etc.)<br>•<br>Documenting how the reviews were<br>completed, including how all BAU<br>activities were verified as being in place.<br>•<br>Collection of documented evidence as<br>required for the annual PCI DSS<br>assessment<br>•<br>Review and sign-off of results by<br>personnel assigned responsibility for the<br>PCI DSS compliance program (as<br>identified in A3.1.3)<br>•<br>Retention of records and documentation<br>for at least 12 months, covering all BAU<br>activities<br>PCI DSS Reference: Requirements 1-12 | Examine policies and procedures to verify that<br>A3.3.3.a<br>processes are defined for reviewing and verifying BAU<br>activities. Verify the procedures include:<br>•<br>Confirming that all BAU activities (e.g., A3.2.2,<br>A3.2.6, and A3.3.1) are being performed<br>•<br>Confirming that personnel are following security<br>policies and operational procedures (for example,<br>daily log reviews, firewall rule-set reviews,<br>configuration standards for new systems, etc.)<br>•<br>Documenting how the reviews were completed,<br>including how all BAU activities were verified as<br>being in place<br>•<br>Collecting documented evidence as required for the<br>annual PCI DSS assessment<br>•<br>Reviewing and sign-off of results by executive<br>management assigned responsibility for PCI DSS<br>governance<br>•<br>Retaining records and documentation for at least 12<br>months, covering all BAU activities<br>Interview responsible personnel and examine<br>A3.3.3.b<br>records of reviews to verify that:<br>•<br>Reviews are performed by personnel assigned to<br>the PCI DSS compliance program.<br>•<br>Reviews are performed at least quarterly. | Implementing PCI DSS controls into business-as<br>usual activities is an effective method to ensure<br>security is included as part of normal business<br>operations on an ongoing basis. Therefore, it is<br>important that independent checks are performed<br>to ensure BAU controls are active and working as<br>intended.<br>The intent of these independent checks is to<br>review the evidence that confirms business-as<br>usual activities are being performed.<br>These reviews can also be used to verify that<br>appropriate evidence is being maintained—for<br>example, audit logs, vulnerability scan reports,<br>firewall reviews, etc.—to assist the entity's<br>preparation for its next PCI DSS assessment. |
